Hands down my favorite first-person shooter of all time. Run-and-gun rambos need not apply. Recoil and ballistics are realistic. Single hits to the chest, neck, or head from rifle caliber weapons almost always result in death, unless it just grazes you. Rounds drop over distance and short, controlled bursts are required if you wish to remain on target. There is no magical ammunition pool. If you reload halfway through your magazine all the time, then you will have a pouch full of half-full mags. You can't run forever. If you sprint for a while, your aim will suffer as you gasp heavily until you have caught your breath. Rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a window sill to steady your aim. Accuracy suffers when standing.

The Conservative Party has proposed a new policing initiative that would empower local communities to directly elect their neighborhood police commissioners. Unveiled by party leader Michael Howard, this plan aims to replace what he described as "overly bureaucratic" police authorities while maintaining the existing role of chief constables. However, the proposal has drawn criticism from multiple fronts. The Liberal Democrats expressed concerns about potential extremist influence over local policing, while Labour dismissed the scheme as another example of Conservative overpromising. Jan Berry, Chair of the Police Federation of England and Wales, emphasized the critical need to preserve operational independence in law enforcement. "Policing is a vital public service, not a political bargaining chip to be exploited during election cycles," Berry cautioned. "There's a real danger these reforms could enable ideologically driven individuals to improperly influence frontline policing decisions." The Federation, representing frontline officers across both nations, stressed the importance of keeping law enforcement free from partisan interference.

Sensational health headlines have become a daily staple, often presenting conflicting claims that can be misleading and confusing. The disconnect between attention-grabbing headlines and the nuanced findings of the underlying medical research can be significant. To critically evaluate the credibility of such headlines, it is essential to examine the original research they are based on. To illustrate this, let's consider three hypothetical research scenarios, each corresponding to a commonly encountered type of headline. By analyzing these examples, we can develop a more informed approach to assessing the validity of health-related news stories.

A critical deadline looms for Fiat and General Motors (GM) as they scramble to resolve a long-standing dispute over a potential acquisition. By midnight on 1 February, the two automotive giants must come to a mutually agreeable solution, or Fiat will be entitled to offload its struggling car division to GM, as stipulated in their 2000 alliance agreement. However, GM, which is grappling with significant losses in its European operations, has grown increasingly reluctant to assume ownership of Fiat's unprofitable unit. The stalled negotiations have taken a toll on Fiat's stock, with shares plummeting 1.2% on Tuesday, following a 4% surge on Monday fueled by speculation of a potential payout. GM is reportedly offering a substantial $2 billion (£1.06 billion) to extricate itself from the arrangement, citing Fiat's decision to divest its finance arm and dilute GM's stake through a capital-raising initiative as grounds for voiding the original deal. The 2000 agreement was the culmination of a fierce bidding war between GM and DaimlerChrysler to secure a strategic partnership with Fiat.

Sun offers processing by the hour sun microsystems has launched a pay-as-you-go service which will allow customers requiring huge computing power to rent it by the hour. Sun grid costs users $1 (53p) for an hour's worth of processing and storage power on systems maintained by sun. So-called grid computing is the latest buzz phrase in a company which believes that computing capacity is as important a commodity as hardware and software. Sun likened grid computing to the development of electricity. The system could mature in the same way utilities such as electricity and water have developed, said sun's chief operating officer jonathan schwartz. "why build your own grid when you can use ours for a buck an hour?" he asked in a webcast launching sun's quarterly network computing event in california.

"There are a lot of 'urban legends' going around these days about Walmart, but facts are facts.

For those who grew up in the UK during the 80s and 90s, this game is a nostalgic dream come true, evoking memories of watching the iconic Transformers cartoon, albeit often as a means to endure the antics of Timmy Mallet. The return of the original voice cast, including the legendary actors behind Optimus Prime, Megatron, and Bumblebee, is a masterstroke, with their instantly recognisable voices transporting players back to a bygone era. The unmistakable sound effect that accompanies the Transformers' signature transformations is also faithfully recreated, adding to the game's retro charm. Visually, the cell-shaded graphics are a perfect homage to the Generation One aesthetic, capturing the essence of the classic cartoon. However, beneath its nostalgic veneer lies a genuinely enjoyable gaming experience. The combat mechanics are expertly crafted, with the ability to transform mid-battle seamlessly integrated into combos, setting it apart from other notable Transformers titles. Notably, the game places a strong emphasis on melee combat, although certain enemies, such as the formidable Seekers, necessitate a tactical switch to ranged attacks, adding a welcome layer of depth to the gameplay.

I can't think of anyone who just has one interest in life, and that's all they want to do for the rest of their life. [the way we work]around 15 percent of american workers don't have traditional full-time jobs. They're half-time, part-time, contract workers or temps. The term "side hustle" just seems to fit with this ethos where people are putting together a few different things to make a living.the word "side hustle" has its roots in popular african american newspapers. In the 1920s, these papers used the word "hustle" to refer to some kind of scam. By the 1950s, they were using "side hustle" to refer to legitimate work, too. A side hustle is a little different than a second job. A second job is about necessity.

This surge underscores blogging's transformative impact on modern digital culture.

We present a new mixed variable symplectic (mvs) integrator for planetary systems, that fully resolve close encounters. The method is based on a time regularisation that allows keeping the stability properties of the symplectic integrators, while also reducing the effective step size whenever two planets encounter. We use a high order mvs scheme such that it is possible to integrate with large time steps far away from close encounters. We show that this algorithm is able to resolve almost exact collisions (i.e with a mutual separation of a fraction of the physical radius) while using the same time-step as in weakly perturbed problem such as the solar system. We demonstrate the long term behaviour on systems of six super-earths experiencing strong scattering for 50 kyr. We compare our algorithm to hybrid methods such as mercury and show that for an equivalent cost we obtain much better energy conservation.

When I look in the mirror today, I see a justice and education scholar at Columbia University, a youth mentor, an activist, and a future New York State senator. I see all of that and a man who spent a quarter of his life in state prison—six years, to be precise—beginning as a teenager on Rikers Island for an act that nearly cost someone their life. However, what brought me from that past to my present wasn't the punishment I faced as a teenager in an adult prison or the severity of our legal system. Rather, it was the educational environment of a classroom that opened my eyes to possibilities I never imagined for myself or the justice system at large. Just a few weeks before my release on parole, a counselor encouraged me to enroll in a new college course being offered within the prison.

And at the time, there wasn't really that much information about the company, but the little that was there was really impressive. Essentially, what the company was doing was creating a medical device where you would be able to run your entire blood panel on a finger-stick of blood. Speaking on BBC Radio 4's Thought for the Day, the Cardinal reiterated his strong opposition to the war, describing it as a "terrible" waste of resources. He joined the Archbishop of Canterbury in speaking out against the conflict, which he believes has diverted funds that could have been used to make a meaningful impact in the lives of those struggling in poverty. The Cardinal urged 2005 to be a year of action, calling for a concerted effort to "make history by ending poverty and making a difference in the lives of those who need it most.

Determining the intrinsic dimensionality of data is a critical preliminary step in dimensionality reduction. This paper presents *intrinsic*, an R package that introduces cutting-edge likelihood-based estimators for assessing dataset dimensionality. Specifically, the package implements three advanced methodologies: the Two-NN, Gride, and HIDALGO models. Designed for accessibility, *intrinsic* offers user-friendly high-level functions supported by optimized computational routines.  

The package features two distinct classes of dimensionality estimators: homogeneous and heterogeneous. The homogeneous category includes the Two-NN and Gride models, which support both frequentist and Bayesian inference. The heterogeneous category comprises HIDALGO, a Bayesian mixture model, accompanied by an efficient Gibbs sampling implementation for robust analysis.

Singer's film to show at festival a documentary which takes a candid look at the life of chart-topping singer george michael will be shown at this year's berlin film festival. A different story will screen in the panorama section of the festival, which runs from 10-20 february. It features the singer talking about both his career and his personal life, from his days in wham! through to more recent events. Michael will attend the festival to introduce the screening on 16 february. Director southan morris and executive producer andy stephens will also attend the festival. The 93 minute film will see michael discussing his early days in wham! along with his later career, including his legal battles with record label sony and his stance against the iraq war and american politics.

While they offer more storywise, the combat can feel sloppy and doesn't feel as sharp as Arkham City. As of writing this recommendation (10/26/2013), there is a game-breaking bug that prevents a side mission from being completed (main story unaffected). Some gadgets are placed at an absurdly hotkey, requiring a significant amount of keyboard travel to access. While it may seem minor, this can lead to frustration in the heat of combat.

Today has been an exhausting yet fulfilling day. After our long journey back from Indiana, I find myself needing to be selective about what I share, as recounting everything would require more time and energy than I currently possess.  

First and foremost, I must express how incredible the camp experience was—it surpassed all my expectations. Recently, God has been working profoundly in my life, and the transformation has been nothing short of remarkable. It feels as though I've started a new chapter.  

To rewind, our trip began when we arrived at the Twiggs' house on Friday evening. We stayed there until Monday, when we departed for camp. During our time with them, we enjoyed a variety of activities, including visiting the fair, attending church, watching a parade, shopping at Walmart, and relaxing with movies. Each moment contributed to an unforgettable experience.

Jason Robinson, the captain of England's rugby union team, is aiming for a significant dual-code victory against Australia this Saturday. Having transitioned from rugby league to rugby union in 2000, Robinson will lead England as they face Australia at Twickenham at 14:30 GMT. Later, at 18:15 GMT, Great Britain's rugby league team will meet Australia in the Tri-Nations tournament final. Robinson stated, "Defeating the Aussies in both matches would be a tremendous accomplishment, particularly for rugby league." England is poised to secure their third autumn international win following successful matches against Canada and South Africa, and they hope to avenge their 51-15 loss to the Wallabies in June. Simultaneously, Great Britain has the opportunity to break a 34-year win drought against Australia with a victory at Elland Road.

A renowned phrase once likened politics to "show business for the aesthetically challenged," and I must say, I feel a deep sense of accomplishment in having reached this milestone. It is a profound privilege for a politician to be invited to deliver a TED Talk, especially in the UK, where the reputation of politics has been severely tarnished by the expenses scandal. The public's perception of politicians has sunk to an all-time low, with some even joking that scientists have considered replacing laboratory rats with politicians, citing an abundance of willing participants, a general apathy towards their well-being, and a certain reluctance on the part of rats to engage in unscrupulous behavior. I regret that I am unable to reveal my identity, as doing so would put me in grave danger. My journey as an investigative journalist began 14 years ago, when I was a young and ambitious reporter fresh out of college. I stumbled upon a groundbreaking scoop, which involved uncovering a widespread phenomenon  police officers accepting bribes from street vendors. As a young journalist, I believed that I could make a meaningful difference by shedding light on this issue, despite the fact that everyone knew it was happening and yet, it was being ignored by the system. I decided to take a bold step and go undercover, adopting the role of a vendor myself. Through this immersive reporting, I was able to gather irrefutable evidence, which had a profound impact. This style of journalism, known as undercover reporting or immersion journalism, allowed me to expose the truth and spark meaningful change.

Yukos, the Russian oil company, will seek court action in the United States on Wednesday to pursue sanctions against Baikal Finance Group, the little-known firm that acquired its main asset. Yukos has announced it will sue Baikal and others involved in the sale of Yuganskneftegas for $20 billion in damages. Yukos' US lawyers will attempt to have Baikal assets frozen after the Russian government ignored a US court order last week blocking the sale. Baikal's background and its motives for purchasing the unit are still unclear. Russian newspapers have reported that Baikal, which acquired the Yuganskneftegas production unit for $9.4 billion (261 billion rubles, £4.8 billion) on Sunday at a state-ordered auction, has strong ties with Surgutneftegas, Russia's fourth-largest oil producer.

Advances in high-resolution optical imaging have been significantly driven by the widespread adoption of nonlinear fluorescence emission techniques. This study focuses on the anomalous saturation behavior of nitrogen vacancy defects in diamond, a phenomenon that has garnered considerable attention in recent years. Notably, our experiments revealed a pronounced reduction in fluorescence emission when subjected to high-power laser excitation, which in turn amplified the nonlinearity of the fluorescence signal and altered the spatial frequency distribution of the resulting fluorescence image. To extract high spatial frequency information, we employed a differential excitation protocol that involved modulating the power of the excitation laser. This approach yielded a substantial improvement in spatial resolution, with an approximate 1.6-fold enhancement compared to conventional confocal microscopy. Given the relative simplicity of the experimental setup and data processing requirements, we anticipate that this method will have far-reaching implications for enhancing the spatial resolution of sensing and biological labeling applications that utilize defects in solid-state materials.

"Halloween screenwriter and producer Debra Hill, best known for her work on the 1978 horror classic Halloween, has died in Los Angeles at the age of 54. Hill, who had been suffering from cancer, co-wrote the film, which starred Jamie Lee Curtis as a babysitter terrorized by a psychopath. Directed by John Carpenter, it grossed over $60 million (£31.3 million) worldwide, a record for an independent film at the time. Hill also collaborated with Carpenter on the films Escape from New York and The Fog. Born in New Jersey, Hill began her career as a production assistant and worked her way up through the ranks, eventually becoming an assistant director and second-unit director. She was widely regarded as a pioneering woman in the film industry, breaking ground in the 1970s by taking on roles that were more commonly held by men.

Arnesen denies rift with santini tottenham sporting director frank arnesen has denied that coach jacques santini resigned because of a clash of personalities at white hart lane. There had been newspaper speculation that santini had felt undermined by arnesen's role at the club. "it is absolutely not true," arnesen told bbc radio five live. "there is only one thing that made him resign and that is his own personal problems. "he has talked to me recently and said this matter is absolutely for himself." arnesen said he was unable to throw any light onto the problems that caused santini to quit after just 13 games in charge. He added: "jacques has never gone into exactly what it was. But i trust him in that; you have to accept it. I think we should respect it.

Economy focus for election battle britain's economic future will be at the heart of labour's poll campaign, chancellor gordon brown has said. He was speaking after cabinet members held their last meeting at no 10 before the expected election announcement. He said voters would recognise that labour had brought stability and growth, and would continue to do so. Meanwhile the tories outlined their plans to tackle "yob culture" and the lib dems gave more details about their proposals to replace council tax. Earlier the archbishop of canterbury wrote to all three parties urging them not to fight the election by exploiting people's fears. In an open letter, he called on them not to turn the election into a competition about who can most effectively frighten voters about terrorism, asylum, and crime.

There is growing discontent within the Cabinet over plans to divert funds from Whitehall to ease the pressure on council tax bills, as acknowledged by Local Government Minister Nick Raynsford. In his pre-budget report on Thursday, Gordon Brown announced the reallocation of £512 million from central to local government budgets. Mr. Raynsford mentioned that he has engaged in "pretty frank discussions" with his fellow ministers regarding these plans. He emphasized the importance of local governments providing quality services without imposing significant increases in council tax. The reallocated funds are part of a £1 billion initiative designed to help local authorities in England maintain council tax increases below 5% in the upcoming year, coinciding with what is anticipated to be a general election year. Mr. Raynsford also noted that nearly all central government departments have a vested interest in ensuring local authorities are well managed.

Entanglement is a key resource for quantum information processing. A widely used tool for detecting entanglement is entanglement witness, where the measurement of the witness operator is guaranteed to be positive for all separable states and can be negative for certain entangled states. In reality, due to the exponentially increasing the hilbert-space dimension with respective to the system size, it is very challenging to construct an efficient entanglement witness for general multipartite entangled states. For $n$-partite greenberger-horne-zeilinger (ghz)-like states, the most robust witness scheme requires $n+1$ local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/2$ white noise. As a comparison, the most efficient witness for ghz-like states only needs two local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/3$ white noise.

**Chinese Dam Builder Challenges Government Directive**  

The China Three Gorges Project Corporation is reportedly defying a government mandate to suspend construction on its massive Xiluodu Dam, according to state media reports. Despite a nationwide halt imposed on 30 major infrastructure projects due to inadequate environmental reviews, the state-owned firm—best known for constructing the Three Gorges Dam—continues work on the Xiluodu project.  

*Beijing News* revealed that the corporation may opt to pay a penalty rather than cease operations. Additionally, the company has disregarded stoppage orders at two other sites: the Three Gorges Underground Power Plant and the Three Gorges Electrical Power Supply Plant.  

China’s State Environmental Protection Administration (SEPA) had flagged these projects for failing to conduct mandatory environmental impact assessments. However, as of now, only 22 of the 30 non-compliant projects have adhered to the shutdown directive.

In 1903, President Theodore Roosevelt embarked on a three-day sojourn in California's majestic Yosemite Valley, where he spent a night beneath the towering canopy of sequoia trees, braved a snowstorm, and engaged in lively conversations around the campfire with his host and guide, the renowned conservationist John Muir. This expedition was not merely a recreational excursion for the great outdoorsman, as Muir had invited him to Yosemite for a pressing purpose  the valley was facing an existential threat. Although Yosemite had been designated as protected land in 1864, it was still vulnerable to overdevelopment in 1903. The struggle to strike a balance between preservation and public use had been ongoing for decades, with the battle for Yosemite's future dating back to the 1849 gold rush, when a surge of miners flooded the Sierra Nevada foothills in search of gold.

City's 0-2 defeat to Manchester United on Sunday has left them nine points behind the leaders in the Premier League, after a scrappy victory for the Red Devils. Wayne Rooney scored the opener with a low shot that deflected off Richard Dunne and into the net, before Steve McManaman wasted a glorious chance to level the score with a shot from three yards out in the first half. Manchester United's approach play was less fluid than patient in the opening 45 minutes, but they managed to fashion just one chance - a Paul Scholes header over the bar. The victory for Manchester United was a significant blow to City's title hopes, as they now trail the leaders by nine points with just 11 games remaining in the season.

Podcasts herald the emergence of DIY radio, with devices like the Apple iPod and other digital music players capable of storing up to 10,000 songs. Many iPod users are increasingly filling this space not just with music but with a diverse range of audio content from various creators. This phenomenon, known as "podcasting," is championed by Adam Curry, a former MTV host and VJ (video jockey). Although the term podcasting originates from the Apple iPod, owning one is not necessary to either create or enjoy a podcast. Essentially, a podcast is an internet-based radio show that podcasters typically produce from the comfort of their own homes. The necessary tools are simply a microphone, a PC, and some editing software. Once created, these shows are uploaded to the internet for others to freely download and enjoy.

If they plan to stay six months or more and are from a country with lots of tb, they would have to have a chest x-ray and further tests if appropriate. Spintronics nanodevices have ultrafast nonlinear dynamic and recurrent behaviors on a nanosecond scale, promising to enable spintronics reservoir computing (RC) systems. Here, two physical RC systems based on a single magnetic skyrmion memristor (MSM) and 24 spin-torque nano-oscillators (STNOS) were proposed and modeled to process image classification tasks and nonlinear dynamic system prediction, respectively. Based on our micromagnetic simulation results on the nonlinear responses of MSM and STNOS with current pulse stimulation, our findings indicate that an RC system using a single MSM has outstanding performance on image classification tasks. Moreover, the complex unknown nonlinear dynamic problems can also be well solved by a physical RC system consisting of 24 STNOS confirmed in a second-order nonlinear dynamic system and NARMA10 tasks.

Nestled within the pristine expanse of Canada's boreal forest—the world's largest and most ecologically intact woodland—lies an environmental and industrial project of unprecedented scale and destruction. Spanning the northern reaches of the country, including Labrador, this region shelters the planet's last great migratory caribou population: the George River herd, with nearly 400,000 individuals. Though I failed to spot a single caribou during my visit, their presence is unmistakably marked by the scattered antlers dotting the landscape.  

The boreal forest is a sanctuary of unparalleled biodiversity, particularly its vast network of wetlands—among Earth's most threatened yet vital ecosystems. These natural wonders serve as the planet's lungs and kidneys, purifying air and water while capturing immense quantities of greenhouse gases. Beyond their ecological functions, they sustain an extraordinary array of wildlife, making them indispensable to the health of our planet.

**Britain's Economic Future Takes Center Stage in Election Campaign**  

Chancellor Gordon Brown has declared that the nation's economic prospects will form the cornerstone of Labour's election strategy. His remarks followed the final cabinet meeting at 10 Downing Street ahead of the anticipated election announcement. Brown emphasized that voters would acknowledge Labour's record of delivering economic stability and sustained growth—a commitment the party pledges to uphold.  

Meanwhile, the Conservatives unveiled initiatives to address antisocial behavior, while the Liberal Democrats expanded on their plans to reform local taxation.  

In a notable intervention, the Archbishop of Canterbury issued an open letter to all major political parties, urging them to refrain from exploiting public anxieties during the campaign. He implored leaders to avoid reducing the election to a contest of fearmongering over issues such as terrorism, immigration, and crime.

We report on the thermally induced transverse vibrations of a photonic crystal waveguide (PCW). The waveguide is composed of two parallel nanobeams separated by a 240 nm vacuum gap. We have developed and validated models to translate the motion of these beams into phase and amplitude modulation of a weak optical probe traveling through a guided mode (GM) in the waveguide. This analysis is applicable for probe frequencies both far from and near the dielectric band edge. The PCW was specifically designed for near-field atom trapping, and this study lays the groundwork for assessing potential adverse effects of thermal motion on optical atomic traps located close to the waveguide surfaces. Our long-term objectives include creating robust atom-mediated interactions between individual vibration phonons and single photons traveling in the PCW’s guided modes, thus paving the way for quantum-level opto-mechanical systems integrating atoms, photons, and phonons.

As of this week, EnronOnline has introduced online USD/CAD currency swap transactions. In my capacity to assess counterparty eligibility for these products, I conduct a comprehensive review that includes:  

1. Consulting the financial trading agreement database for any stated restrictions  
2. Reviewing the master swap agreement terms  
3. Examining all board resolutions submitted during the master agreement execution process  

My analysis has revealed a historical pattern: many older board resolutions authorizing master agreements and derivative transactions explicitly excluded currency trading permissions. Given that energy-sector currency swaps represent a new business line for Enron, I urge all teams to:  

- Thoroughly verify legacy resolutions rather than relying exclusively on Lotus Notes restriction records  
- Exercise due diligence given this operational expansion into foreign exchange markets  

This proactive approach will help mitigate authorization risks as we develop this service offering.

With just hours to go until the midnight deadline on February 1, Fiat and General Motors (GM) are in a race against time to settle their contentious takeover dispute. The impending deadline marks the point at which Fiat will gain the right to sell its car division to GM, as per the alliance agreed upon in 2000. However, GM, whose European operations have been losing money, is no longer interested in owning the unprofitable Fiat unit. Reports of stalled negotiations have sent Fiat shares plummeting 1.2% on Tuesday, following a 4% gain on Monday, fueled by hopes of a resolution. GM is believed to be offering around $2 billion to extricate itself from the arrangement, citing Fiat's decision to divest its finance arm and halve GM's stake through a capital-raising initiative as the deal's voiding factor. The 2000 deal was the result of a high-stakes game of cat and mouse between GM and DaimlerChrysler, as both companies sought to form an alliance with Fiat.

The starving orphan seeking a second helping of gruel. The spinster wasting away in her tattered wedding dress. The stone-hearted miser plagued by the ghost of christmas past. More than a century after his death, these remain recognizable figures from the work of charles dickens. So striking is his body of work that it gave rise to its own adjective. But what are the features of dickens's writing that make it so special?dickens’s fiction brims with anticipation through brooding settings, plot twists, and mysteries. These features of his work kept his audience wanting more. When first published, his stories were serialized, meaning they were released a few chapters at a time in affordable literary journals and only later reprinted as books.

The original band members, Bryan Ferry, Phil Manzerana, Andy McKay, and Paul Thompson, will perform on stage on the Saturday night of the festival. This groundbreaking paper introduces a cutting-edge, millimeter-scale CMOS 64x64 single charged particle radiation detector system designed specifically for external beam cancer radiotherapy applications. A 1x1 μm² diode precisely measures the energy deposition by a single charged particle within the depletion region, while the array's innovative design enables a substantial detection area of 512x512 μm². Contrary to traditional methods that rely on sensing the voltage drop caused by radiation, this novel system leverages the pulse width measurement, namely the time it takes for the voltage to return to its baseline. This approach eliminates the need for power-hungry and large analog-to-digital converters, thereby significantly reducing power consumption. A prototype ASIC was fabricated using the TSMC 65 nm LP CMOS process and exhibits an average static power consumption of 0.535 mW under a 1.2 V analog and digital power supply. The system's functionality has been successfully validated in a clinical 67.5 MeV proton beam setting, demonstrating its efficacy in real

Yukos Returns to U.S. Courts in Battle for Survival  

The embattled Russian oil and gas giant Yukos is set to appear before a U.S. court this Thursday as part of its ongoing struggle to avoid collapse. Facing a staggering $27 billion tax claim, the company is being dismantled by Russian authorities.  

In a strategic move, Yukos sought U.S. bankruptcy protection, aiming to leverage international legal frameworks to block the forced sale of its crown jewel—the Yuganskneftegas oil production unit. Despite these efforts, the unit was ultimately acquired by state-owned Rosneft for $9.4 billion, though not before the auction faced significant disruptions.  

Yukos and its legal team now contend that the sale violated U.S. bankruptcy statutes. The company and its principal shareholders have pledged to pursue all available legal avenues against any entities involved in acquiring its assets, signaling an unrelenting fight for justice.

I'd like to share with you an intriguing glimpse into the future of money, beginning with a fascinating story from a culture in Micronesia known as the Yap, dating back to the early 1900s. The Yap people had a unique form of currency: enormous limestone discs called rai stones. These discs weren't exchanged or physically moved like our coins because they could be incredibly large, with the biggest weighing around four tons and measuring up to 12 feet in diameter. Instead, the Yap maintained a collective memory of who owned shares in each stone. There's a tale of sailors transporting a rai stone across the sea when it unfortunately sank to the ocean floor. Despite the stone's loss, its ownership continued to be acknowledged by the community.

The notion of criminalizing flag desecration struck me as intriguing—perhaps even a potential defense against accusations of witchcraft. As an international blogger, I’ve generally hesitated to engage in certain cultural debates, especially those involving national symbols. However, having initiated this discourse (the conversation itself, not the act of burning flags), I feel compelled to share my perspective.  

Fundamentally, legislating against flag-burning is absurd. When we grant any symbol—regardless of its significance—such unquestioning veneration and legal safeguarding, we inch dangerously closer to authoritarianism. Symbols are inherently mutable; their power lies not in their intrinsic value but in the meanings we ascribe to them or impose upon them.

Warning over tsunami aid website net users are being told to avoid a scam website that claims to collect cash on behalf of tsunami victims. The site looks plausible because it uses an old version of the official disasters emergency committee webpage. However, dec has no connection with the fake site and says it has contacted the police about it. The site is just the latest in a long list of scams that try to cash in on the goodwill generated by the tsunami disaster. The link to the website is contained in a spam e-mail that is currently circulating. The message's subject line reads "urgent tsunami earthquake appeal" and its text bears all the poor grammar and bad spelling that characterises many other phishing attempts.

My journey to become a polar specialist, photographing, specializing in the polar regions, began when i was four years old, when my family moved from southern canada to northern baffin island, up by greenland. There we lived with the inuit in the tiny inuit community of 200 inuit people, where [we] were one of three non-inuit families. And in this community, we didn't have a television; we didn't have computers, obviously, radio. We didn't even have a telephone. All of my time was spent outside with the inuit, playing. The snow and the ice were my sandbox, and the inuit were my teachers. And that's where i became truly obsessed with this polar realm.

Every day, health headlines capture our attention with claims like, "A single vitamin can lower your risk of heart disease," or "Eating chocolate reduces stress among students." We also hear about breakthroughs like, "New drug extends lives of patients with rare diseases." However, these headlines often contradict each other or oversimplify the complex findings of the underlying research. This discrepancy arises because catchy headlines tend to overlook the nuanced and incremental nature of scientific studies. How can you ensure that you’re not misled by sensationalistic headlines? The key is to examine the original research that the headline is based on. We've crafted hypothetical research scenarios to illustrate this point for each of these headlines. Stay tuned for an explanation of the first example, and take a moment to reflect when you see the headline. These scenarios are simplified for clarity.

The dichotomy between two twin domes, one crafted from thousands of steel components and the other from a single silk thread, highlights the fundamental contrast between synthetic and organic design approaches. One is imposed upon the environment, while the other creates it. Michelangelo once gazed upon raw marble, he saw a figure struggling to be set free. The chisel was Michelangelo's only tool. However, living things do not conform to the chisel. They grow. At the most fundamental level, our cells carry all the information necessary for every other cell to function and replicate. Similarly, tools also have consequences. Since the industrial revolution, the world of design has been dominated by the rigors of manufacturing and mass production, leading to a profound impact on the way we approach design. This has resulted in a world where the boundaries between creator and creation are increasingly blurred, and the value of human craftsmanship is often overlooked.

Traditional understanding suggests that an electron primarily exhibits a single form of kinetic energy. However, could it possess additional variations related to its spin, charge, or interactions with other electrons? In one-dimensional (1D) systems, the Tomonaga-Luttinger-Liquid (TLL) model accurately describes the behavior of interacting electrons at low energies, though experimental observations beyond this linear range have been limited. In this study, we present our findings on many-body modes in 1D gated wires through tunneling spectroscopy techniques. We detected two distinct Fermi seas at elevated energies, corresponding to spin and charge excitations, along with the development of three additional 1D 'replica' modes that become more pronounced with shorter wire lengths. The effective interaction strength within the wires was modified by adjusting the degree of 1D inter-subband screening, exceeding a 45% change.

The spreadsheet below provides an implicit commodity breakdown based on the effective date of four different rates for the same rate schedule (schedule DR). **Subject ** Legislative Update on SoCal MOU Bill  

Governor Davis and Senator Polanco reached an informal agreement over the weekend, in which Polanco has committed to sponsoring the Southern California Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) bill. Polanco, a Democratic senator currently embroiled in a personal scandal, is widely believed to be at the end of his political career. Rather than seeking re-election to the Senate, he intends to pursue a mayoral bid in Los Angeles.  

The bill is anticipated to be introduced in the newly convened extraordinary session, with proceedings expected to begin imminently. Once introduced, it will first be referred to the Senate Energy Committee. However, given that Committee Chair Senator Bowen is a staunch opponent of the MOU, the bill is likely to face prolonged deliberations in committee. Extended negotiations and amendments could further delay its progress, increasing the risk of a Southern California bankruptcy.

A recent study inspired by Monte Carlo experiments (Jiménez, Journal of Turbulence, 2020) reveals that dipoles play a crucial role in the dynamics of decaying two-dimensional turbulence, rivaling the importance of individual vortex cores. Notably, the kinetic energy of this flow is predominantly carried by elongated streams formed through the concatenation of dipoles. The vortices in this system undergo a bifurcation, separating into two distinct families: small, rapidly moving cores and larger, slowly moving ones that become 'frozen' into a slowly evolving, crystalline structure. The kinematic properties of these two families exhibit stark differences, with only the smaller, faster-moving vortices displaying self-similarity. Conversely, the larger, slower-moving vortices are responsible for the majority of the flow's kinetic energy and are the primary constituents of the dipoles and streams. The underlying mechanisms driving the growth of this slow-moving component are explored in detail.

Where does the carbon dioxide we release go? Carbon plays a crucial role in a natural cycle, present in all living organisms  in the soil, in the oceans, in the atmosphere, and in rocks deep beneath the Earth's surface. When plants and other living organisms die, a significant portion of their stored carbon is released back to the atmosphere, where it is absorbed by new plants and microorganisms, perpetuating the cycle. However, over millions of years, some of the carbon stored in ancient trees, sea life, and other organisms becomes trapped in rock formations, eventually transforming into fossil fuels  coal, oil, and natural gas. Natural geological events, such as volcanic eruptions, release some of this trapped carbon, while human activities, like the burning of fossil fuels, also release a significant amount of prehistoric carbon dioxide. In fact, human activities today release approximately 60 times more carbon dioxide than all the volcanic eruptions on the planet each year, highlighting the profound impact of human activities on the global carbon cycle.

The Weak Gravity Conjecture (WGC) posits that in any consistent quantum theory of gravity, there must exist superextremal particles. Conventionally, these particles are thought to ensure that extremal black holes are either unstable or only marginally stable. However, the consequences of this not being the case are not entirely clear. This note demonstrates that in a general Einstein quantum theory of gravity within Anti-de Sitter (AdS) space, the precise stability of extremal black branes conflicts with established quantum information theorems concerning entanglement entropy. To resolve this contradiction, the note introduces a nonperturbative version of the WGC, which aligns with the traditional interpretation in the context of weak coupling. This argument is broad, requiring neither supersymmetry nor a specific ultraviolet (UV) completion. It relies solely on the principles of Einstein gravity, effective field theory, and holography.

Yukos heading back to us courts russian oil and gas company yukos is due in a us court on thursday as it continues to fight for its survival. The firm is in the process of being broken up by russian authorities in order to pay a $27bn (£14bn) tax bill. Yukos filed for bankruptcy in the us, hoping to use international business law to halt the forced sale of its key oil production unit, yuganskneftegas. The unit was however sold for $9.4bn to state oil firm rosneft but only after the state auction had been disrupted. Yukos lawyers now say the auction violated us bankruptcy law. The company and its main shareholders have vowed to go after any company that buys its assets, using all and every legal means.

Subject: Notice of CFTC Approval of New NYMEX Rule 6.40C

Date: October 3, 2000

To all NYMEX and COMEX Members,

From: Neal L. Wolkoff, Executive Vice President

I am writing to inform you that the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has approved the implementation of NYMEX Rule 6.40C, titled "Average Price Calculations by Futures Commission Merchants." This newly approved rule, which aligns with a recent CFTC advisory, is now in effect. 

Rule 6.40C allows Futures Commission Merchants (FCMs), under certain specified conditions, to utilize their own internal, proprietary systems for generating Average Price (AP) calculations. This applies when multiple prices are received on a single order or a series of orders. The full details of this rule are attached for your reference. 

Please review this information carefully to ensure compliance. 

Thank you.

In a desperate bid for survival, the accused were faced with an impossible dilemma: confess to a crime they did not commit, or risk a gruesome public execution. The year was 1692, and the village of Salem, Massachusetts, was gripped by a frenzy of paranoia and hysteria, fueled by a toxic mix of superstition, misguided zealotry, and a flawed justice system that prioritized repentance over truth. For the victims of this witch-hunt, the consequences of maintaining their innocence were dire, while a false confession, accompanied by a contrite apology and the incrimination of others, offered a perverse guarantee of freedom. This was the cruel reality that confronted the people of Salem, a community founded by English Puritans in 1626, who had established a strict and isolated way of life. The village's history was marked by frequent clashes with Native American tribes and French settlers, a legacy of violence and mistrust that would ultimately contribute to the devastating events that unfolded.

More women are turning to net security measures, particularly older people and women, as they take charge of protecting their home computers against malicious net attacks, according to a two-year study. The number of women buying programs to protect PCs from viruses, spam, and spyware rose by 11.2% annually between 2002 and 2004. The study, conducted by a net security firm, shows that security messages are reaching a diverse range of surfers. It is believed that 40% of those buying home net security programs are retired. For the last three years, this figure has risen by an average of 13.2%. However, more retired women (53%) are buying security software than retired men. The research reflects the evolving stereotype and demographics of web users, as well as the growing awareness of the greater risks that high-speed broadband net connections can pose to surfers.

Electric network frequency (enf) fluctuations constitute a powerful tool in multimedia forensics. An efficient approach for enf estimation is introduced with temporal windowing based on the filter-bank capon spectral estimator. A type of gohberg-semencul factorization of the model covariance matrix is used due to the toeplitz structure of the covariance matrix. Moreover, this approach uses, for the first time in the field of enf, a temporal window, not necessarily the rectangular one, at the stage preceding spectral estimation. Krylov matrices are employed for fast implementation of matrix inversions. The proposed approach outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in enf estimation, when a short time window of $1$ second is employed in power recordings. In speech recordings, the proposed approach yields highly accurate results with respect to both time complexity and accuracy.

Weight pruning is recognized as a simple yet effective technique for eliminating redundancy in deep neural networks (DNNs), offering the potential for acceleration across multiple platforms. However, many pruning methods compromise between model accuracy and regularity, often hindering inference accuracy and limiting acceleration performance on devices. To address this issue, we introduce a novel sparsity dimension: pattern-based sparsity, which includes both pattern and connectivity sparsity, ensuring high accuracy and hardware compatibility. By using meticulously crafted patterns, our pruning method consistently enhances accuracy and improves feature extraction across various DNN architectures and datasets. Additionally, our pattern-aware pruning framework enables simultaneous library extraction, pattern selection, pattern and connectivity pruning, and weight training. This pattern-based sparsity approach is seamlessly integrated into compiler optimization, enabling highly efficient DNN execution on mobile platforms.

While I can't personally endorse this game, it isn't without merit. The core shooting mechanics are decent—provided you're willing to spend on weapon upgrades, which highlights my primary criticism: its pay-to-win structure. Beyond this flaw, the game has redeeming qualities—the hardsuit mechanics are engaging, and the HRV system is a brilliant innovation. I'd choose this over any Call of Duty installment without hesitation, were it not for its microtransaction-driven progression. If pay-to-win mechanics don't bother you, then dive in and enjoy. But if, like me, you prefer fair competition over wallet-driven advantages, you may want to pass—this simply isn't our style.

Hollywood luminaries Kevin Spacey and Kate Bosworth graced the red carpet at the UK premiere of *Beyond the Sea* in London's Leicester Square on Thursday evening. Spacey, 45, not only stars in the biographical drama but also serves as its writer and director, bringing to life the extraordinary story of legendary 1950s crooner Bobby Darin.  

"*Beyond the Sea* is my heartfelt homage to a truly extraordinary artist," Spacey remarked, embodying Darin’s persona so fully that he performs all 18 tracks on the film’s soundtrack. Meanwhile, Bosworth, 21, portrays Darin’s real-life wife, Hollywood icon Sandra Dee.  

"Before this project, I was completely unfamiliar with Bobby Darin’s legacy, but now I’m utterly captivated by his brilliance," shared Bosworth, who arrived at the premiere alongside her British partner, actor Orlando Bloom. The film marks a passionate tribute to Darin’s enduring influence on music and cinema.

The convergence of interfacial spin-orbit coupling and Josephson physics in a single device presents a fascinating paradigm, enabling the integration of anomalous Hall and Josephson effects in superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. Our theoretical investigation delves into the interplay between these phenomena, revealing a profound impact on the transport properties of such junctions. The transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of Cooper pairs across the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface generates substantial transverse Hall supercurrents, which we term anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. By extending the Furusaki-Tsukada formula, originally developed to quantify conventional Josephson current flows, we demonstrate the ability to evaluate and modulate the transverse current components through the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. Notably, the formation of well-localized subgap bound states around the interface serves as a distinct spectroscopic signature of Josephson junctions, offering a unique probe into the underlying physics.

A novel, conserved current is introduced within the framework of an effective theory encompassing pions and vector mesons, grounded in the concept of hidden local symmetry. This current's associated charge is demonstrated to be equivalent to the skyrmion charge for all smooth configurations, while singular configurations can be identified as baryons with n_f=1, exhibiting charge under the newly proposed symmetry. Notably, vector mesons assume the role of Chern-Simons vector fields residing on the quantum Hall droplet that constitutes the n_f=1 baryon. We propose that this current provides the accurate effective representation of the baryon current at low energies, thereby offering a unified paradigm for the two distinct types of baryons. This unified framework enables a seamless transformation between the two baryon types, while Chern-Simons dualities on the droplet can be interpreted as a manifestation of Seiberg-like duality between gluons and vector mesons, fostering a deeper understanding of the intricate relationships between these fundamental particles.

Recent breakthroughs in optomechanics and electromechanics have enabled the preparation of macroscopic mechanical oscillators in a range of quantum states, including the quantum ground state, quadrature squeezed states, and entangled states of motion. This capability has not only pushed the boundaries of quantum behavior to larger, more tangible objects, but also sparked innovative ideas for harnessing mechanical oscillators in quantum information processing and communication, as well as in the development of precision force sensors that transcend the standard quantum limit. However, the inherently linear interaction between motion and light or electricity has limited access to a broader spectrum of quantum states of motion, such as cat states and energy squeezed states. To overcome this limitation, early optomechanical proposals envisioned the creation of strong quadratic coupling between motion and light, which would unlock a wider range of quantum states. While experimental demonstrations of quadratically coupled optomechanical systems have been successful, they have yet to achieve the milestone of accessing nonclassical states of motion.

Density-based clustering operates on the principle of associating groups with distinct characteristics of the underlying data distribution. By grounding the analysis in an assumed but unobserved population structure, the clustering task can be formalized within a traditional statistical inference framework. Here, the optimal clustering is defined by the true density function’s inherent partitioning.  

A nonparametric implementation of this method, termed *modal clustering*, establishes a direct relationship between clusters and the regions of attraction around density peaks. Practically, this requires estimating the density nonparametrically, where the choice of smoothing—critical in shaping both the density and its modal features—plays a decisive role in determining the final cluster configuration.  

This study examines the problem of density estimation for modal clustering through an asymptotic lens, exploring its theoretical foundations and implications.

Investigating the interplay between a time-reversal symmetry broken Weyl semimetal and an s-wave superconductor, we uncover the intricate transport properties and superconducting proximity effect in nsn junctions. Notably, the differential conductance and induced pairing amplitudes exhibit a pronounced dependence on the angular alignment between the junction's spatial orientation and the momentum-space axis separating the Weyl nodes. Furthermore, our analysis reveals the significant impact of a chiral chemical potential, arising from the electron population imbalance between Weyl nodes of opposing chirality, on the junction's transport characteristics. A striking observation is the emergence of a net spin polarization in Cooper pairs generated through Andreev reflection in the two Weyl semimetal regions, with the polarization being oppositely directed in each region and exhibiting high sensitivity to both the chirality imbalance and excitation energy.

Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in Josephson junctions presents an exciting opportunity to integrate anomalous Hall and Josephson phenomena within a single device. We conduct a theoretical analysis to explore how the interplay of these effects influences the transport properties of superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. The skew tunneling of Cooper pairs, dependent on transverse momentum, across the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface, leads to significant transverse Hall supercurrents. We refer to these as anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. We extend the Furusaki-Tsukada formula—originally established to measure conventional Josephson current flows via tunneling—to assess the transverse current components. Our findings reveal that the amplitudes of these currents can be significantly tuned through the strength of spin-orbit coupling or by altering the superconducting phase difference across the junction. Additionally, as a distinct spectroscopic signature of Josephson junctions, well-defined subgap bound states emerge around the interface.

Optomechanics and electromechanics have made it possible to prepare macroscopic mechanical oscillators in their quantum ground states, in quadrature squeezed states, and in entangled states of motion. In addition to coaxing ever larger and more tangible objects into a regime of quantum behavior, this new capability has encouraged ideas of using mechanical oscillators in the processing and communication of quantum information and as precision force sensors operating beyond the standard quantum limit. But the effectively linear interaction between motion and light or electricity precludes access to the broader class of quantum states of motion, such as cat states or energy squeezed states. Indeed, early optomechanical proposals noted the possibility to escape this restriction by creating strong quadratic coupling of motion to light. Although there have been experimental demonstrations of quadratically coupled optomechanical systems, these have not yet accessed nonclassical states of motion.

Nonlinear fluorescence emission has become a valuable tool for achieving high spatial resolution in optical imaging. In this study, we explored the fluorescence anomalous saturating effect associated with nitrogen vacancy defects in diamond. When exposed to high-power laser excitation, we observed a reduction in fluorescence that increased the nonlinearity of the emission. This change altered the spatial frequency distribution of the resulting fluorescence image. To capture detailed high spatial frequency information, we employed a differential excitation protocol, which involved modulating the power of the excitation laser. This approach enhanced the spatial resolution of imaging by approximately 1.6 times compared to conventional confocal microscopy. Owing to the straightforward nature of both the experimental setup and data processing, we anticipate that this technique will be highly beneficial for increasing the spatial resolution in applications such as sensing and biological labeling using defects in solids.

In 2004, Prince reigned supreme as the highest-earning musician, surpassing industry icons like Madonna and Elton John, according to *Rolling Stone* magazine's annual rankings. The legendary artist amassed an impressive $56.5 million (£30.4 million) through his *Musicology* tour, album sales, and publishing royalties. Madonna secured the second spot with $54.9 million (£29.5 million), fueled by her ambitious *Re-Invention* world tour. Meanwhile, the iconic duo Simon & Garfunkel claimed tenth place, generating $24.9 million (£13.4 million) from their highly anticipated reunion tour.  

*Rolling Stone* noted Prince's triumphant return to the spotlight after years of relative obscurity in the mainstream music scene. His 2004 tour alone grossed $90.3 million (£48.5 million) in ticket sales, while his latest album, *Musicology*, sold 1.9 million copies, cementing his resurgence as a dominant force in the industry.

At age fourteen, my family was navigating the complex journey of adopting my younger brothers from Ethiopia. One day, my mother posed a seemingly simple question: "What date should we list as their birthday?" My initial reaction was dismissive—"The day they were born, obviously." It struck me as an absurd question—until she explained, "Kristen, neither of your brothers has a birth certificate. How would you propose we determine that?"  

That moment reshaped my understanding. Two decades later, I’m still pursuing answers—not just for my brothers, but on a global scale. The absence of birth certificates isn’t merely a personal hurdle; it’s a systemic issue affecting millions. So, why does this matter for international development? The answer lies in the invisible barriers faced by those without legal recognition—access to education, healthcare, and basic rights hinges on something as fundamental as proof of birth.

Weight pruning has been widely acknowledged as a straightforward and effective method to eliminate redundancy in deep neural networks (dnn), thereby achieving acceleration on various platforms. However, most of the pruning techniques are essentially trade-offs between model accuracy and regularity which lead to impaired inference accuracy and limited on-device acceleration performance. To solve the problem, we introduce a new sparsity dimension, namely pattern-based sparsity that comprises pattern and connectivity sparsity, and becoming both highly accurate and hardware friendly. With carefully designed patterns, the proposed pruning unprecedentedly and consistently achieves accuracy enhancement and better feature extraction ability on different dnn structures and datasets, and our pattern-aware pruning framework also achieves pattern library extraction, pattern selection, pattern and connectivity pruning and weight training simultaneously. Our approach on the new pattern-based sparsity naturally fits into compiler optimization for highly efficient dnn execution on mobile platforms.

However, it's not without its drawbacks, including sub-par AI teammates, a limited selection of equipment types, and a linear leveling system with little branching. Despite these limitations, the game still offers a fun and engaging experience. The fast-paced action and constant sense of progression keep players invested, with the promise of new ships and skills to unlock and level up. To truly unlock the full potential of this game, mods are essential. The space combat is undoubtedly the main attraction, but the intense battles for resources are also a major draw. Overall, this game is a great starting point for fans of the genre, and with the right mods, it can become an even more enjoyable experience.

Spintronic nanodevices have ultrafast nonlinear dynamic and recurrence behaviors on a nanosecond scale that promises to enable spintronic reservoir computing (rc) system. Here two physical rc systems based on a single magnetic skyrmion memristor (msm) and 24 spin-torque nano-oscillators (stnos) were proposed and modeled to process image classification task and nonlinear dynamic system prediction, respectively. Based on our micromagnetic simulation results on the nonlinear responses of msm and stno with current pulses stimulation, the handwritten digits recognition task domesticates that an rc system using one single msm has the outstanding performance on image classification. In addition, the complex unknown nonlinear dynamic problems can also be well solved by a physical rc system consisted of 24 stnos confirmed in a second-order nonlinear dynamic system and narma10 tasks.

The study of orientational dynamics in the isotropic phase of a comb-shaped nematic polymer, featuring both mesogenic and functional side groups, utilized the Kerr effect and dielectric spectroscopy for analysis. This investigation revealed, for the first time, a distinct behavior in mesogenic polymers compared to low-molecular-weight mesogens: the relaxation of electric birefringence in a melt above the nematic-isotropic phase transition temperature can be described by a combination of multiple exponential processes, with two processes playing a crucial role. These primary processes take turns dominating across a temperature range of approximately fifty degrees. Dielectric spectroscopy also identified two types of orientational relaxation processes: one arising from the rotation of side mesogenic groups and the other from the movement of main chain segments.

In a striking juxtaposition, two twin domes embody the dichotomy of design philosophies. One, a marvel of modern engineering, comprises thousands of intricately assembled steel components, while the other, a masterpiece of organic elegance, is crafted from a single, unbroken silk thread. This contrast highlights the fundamental differences between synthetic and organic design, with one imposing its presence on the environment and the other harmoniously integrating with it. The former is a product of human ingenuity, carefully designed to coexist with nature, whereas the latter is an expression of nature's inherent design, shaped by its own intrinsic forces.

This dichotomy is reminiscent of Michelangelo's profound observation that, when gazing upon unshaped marble, he envisioned a figure yearning to be liberated. The renowned artist's chisel was the instrument that freed the captive form, but living organisms defy such rigid manipulation. Instead, they grow and evolve, their cells carrying the genetic blueprint that governs their function, replication, and intricate interconnectedness.

The tools we employ in design have far-reaching consequences, and since the advent of the Industrial Revolution, the design world has been largely dictated by the imperatives of mass production and manufacturing.

Andrea Levy's novel "Small Island" has emerged victorious in the Whitbread Novel of the Year category, surpassing Booker Prize winner Alan Hollinghurst's "The Line of Beauty". This triumph positions Levy as the frontrunner for the overall Whitbread Book of the Year award. Meanwhile, Geraldine McCaughrean has secured her third Children's Fiction award for "Not the End of the World". As category winners, these authors will proceed to compete for the prestigious Whitbread Book of the Year title, accompanied by a £25,000 prize. A distinguished panel of judges, comprising Sir Trevor McDonald, Hugh Grant, and Joanne Harris, will convene on January 25 to determine the ultimate winner, with the announcement scheduled for the same evening. Bookmaker William Hill has accordingly installed Levy's novel as the 6/4 favourite to claim the top honour.

Disappointed scott in solid start allan scott is confident of winning a medal at next week's european indoor championships after a solid debut on the international circuit. "it was definitely a learning curve and i certainly haven't ruled out challenging for a medal next week," said the east kilbride athlete. The race was won by felipe vivancos, who equalled the spanish record. Sweden's robert kronberg was second, with haiti's dudley dorival in third. Scott was slightly disappointed with his run in the final. He won his heat in 7.64secs but ran 0.04secs slower in his first iaaf indoor grand prix circuit final.

A comprehensive analysis of the phenomenological implications of a hypercharge-zero SU(2) triplet scalar is presented, driven by the theoretical framework of two-step electroweak symmetry-breaking. Two distinct scenarios are explored: the first, where the triplet scalar is stable and contributes to the dark matter relic density, and the second, where it decays through mixing with the Standard Model Higgs boson. The stable triplet scenario is subject to stringent constraints from disappearing charged track searches at the Large Hadron Collider and direct detection experiments, while the decaying triplet scenario is constrained by existing multilepton searches at colliders. Our findings indicate that a two-step electroweak phase transition involving a stable triplet scalar with a negative quadratic term is incompatible with direct detection searches, whereas an unstable triplet scalar with a mass below 230 GeV is excluded at a 95% confidence level, thereby providing significant insights into the viability of this theoretical framework.

There are currently hundreds of thousands of people on transplant lists, waiting for critical organs like kidneys, hearts, and livers that could save their lives. Unfortunately, there aren’t nearly enough donor organs available to fill that demand.what if instead of waiting, we could create brand-new, customized organs from scratch? that’s the idea behind bioprinting, a branch of regenerative medicine currently under development. We’re not able to print complex organs just yet, but simpler tissues including blood vessels and tubes responsible for nutrient and waste exchange are already in our grasp.bioprinting is a biological cousin of 3-d printing, a technique that deposits layers of material on top of each other to construct a three-dimensional object one slice at a time.

Mefin Davies, the prospective future hooker for Wales, is probably going to continue with Gloucester, the English team, despite interest from the Neath-Swansea Ospreys. BBC Wales reports that although the Ospreys are keen on the 32-year-old player, Davies would rather remain at Gloucester. Davies, who shone in Wales' RBS Six Nations victory over England on Saturday, currently has a one-year contract with Kingsholm. However, he has shown his value to the Zurich Premiership team, making it likely that he will receive a contract extension next season. Following the dissolution of the Celtic Warriors, Davies found himself without a team and had to sign a semi-professional contract with Neath RFC. While he did get playing time with the Ospreys per the Wales management's request, he expressed his frustration with his treatment before joining Gloucester.

Michael Stipe, the lead singer of the band R.E.M., will be attending the Berlin Film Festival this year to screen a documentary about his life and career. The film, titled "Michael Stipe: A Life in Three Acts," will be shown in the Panorama section of the festival, which runs from February 10-20. The documentary will feature Stipe discussing his life and career, from his early days in the band R.E.M. to more recent events. Stipe will introduce the screening on February 16. Directors Southan Morris and Executive Producer Andy Stephens will also attend the festival. The 93-minute film will see Stipe discussing his early days in R.E.M., as well as his later career, including his legal battles with record label Sony and his stance against the Iraq War and American politics.

Man city 0-2 man utd manchester united reduced chelsea's premiership lead to nine points after a scrappy victory over manchester city. Wayne rooney met gary neville's cross to the near post with a low shot, which went in via a deflection off richard dunne, to put united ahead. Seven minutes later, the unfortunate dunne hooked a volley over david james' head and into his own net. Steve mcmanaman wasted city's best chance when he shot wide from three yards in the first half. Mutation-based fuzzing typically uses an initial set of non-crashing seed inputs (a corpus) from which to generate new inputs by mutation. A corpus of potential seeds will often contain thousands of similar inputs. This lack of diversity can lead to wasted fuzzing effort by exhaustive mutation from all available seeds. To address this, fuzzers come with distillation tools (e.g., afl-cmin) that select the smallest subset of seeds that triggers the same range of instrumentation data points as the full corpus. Common practice suggests that minimizing the number and cumulative size of the seeds leads to more efficient fuzzing, which we explore systematically. We present results of 34+ cpu-years of fuzzing with five distillation approaches to understand their impact in finding bugs in real-world software.

The controlled implantation of metal ions into ceramic substrates has proven to be a highly effective method for precisely modifying surface electrical conductivity, with widespread practical applications. This technique is particularly valuable for engineering voltage gradients in high-voltage insulators, significantly enhancing their operational limits by preventing surface flashover. While promising, a critical consideration for this method's viability is the durability of the conductivity modifications, particularly when using oxidation-prone metal species. Our investigation focuses on the temporal evolution of surface conductivity in titanium-implanted alumina ceramics. The findings reveal an initial conductivity reduction of up to 40% within weeks post-implantation, followed by a remarkably stable period with merely a 10% decline over the subsequent year.

The past weekend was a respite of unbridled indulgence, marked by a long-overdue reunion with the soothing allure of nature and the sonic landscapes of Nevermore, a band whose discography I had inexplicably neglected for far too long. This period of relaxation also afforded me the opportunity to devour a substantial amount of literature, culminating in the completion of three novels: Bruce Sterling's 'Heavy Weather', Larry Niven's 'World of Ptavvs', and Octavia Butler's 'Parable of the Sower'. Of these, 'Heavy Weather' proved to be a captivating cyberpunk escapade, set against the backdrop of a dystopian near-future United States ravaged by the devastating consequences of global warming and societal chaos. While Sterling's masterful storytelling and meticulous world-building rendered the narrative both convincing and engaging, I couldn't help but feel that the experience, though enjoyable, was somewhat lacking in depth and emotional resonance. The characters, though skillfully crafted and vividly realized, failed to leave an indelible mark on my psyche, leaving me to conclude that, although 'Heavy Weather' is undoubtedly a well-crafted novel, it falls short of being truly exceptional.

Deep learning has resolved numerous challenges that were previously unreachable for heuristic algorithms. It has also been successfully applied in wireless communications, despite the fact that the current radio systems are well-understood and optimal algorithms exist for many tasks. While some progress has been made by learning individual components of a receiver, a more effective approach is to jointly learn the entire receiver. However, this often results in a complex nonlinear problem, for which an optimal solution is impractical to implement. To address this, we propose a deep fully convolutional neural network, Deeprx, which executes the entire receiver pipeline from frequency domain signal stream to uncoded bits in a 5G-compliant manner. We enhance channel estimation by crafting the input of the convolutional neural network in a carefully designed manner using both data and pilot symbols.

A novel approach to mitigating the adverse effects of selfish routing on network efficiency in autonomous vehicle systems is presented, building upon the concept of routing controllability. However, the conventional assumption that autonomous vehicles would willingly submit to external control by a central authority is overly simplistic. This paper introduces a pioneering joint routing and pricing control framework, designed to motivate autonomous vehicles to adopt system-optimal routing strategies by offering toll savings, while simultaneously imposing congestion charges on user equilibrium-seeking autonomous vehicles and human-driven vehicles. This complex problem is formulated as a bi-level optimization paradigm, wherein dynamic tolls are optimized at the upper level, and a mixed equilibrium simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model is employed at the lower level to account for the interactions between autonomous and human-driven vehicles in a mixed fleet environment.

Low-symmetry 2D materials, including resorbed and reshifted monolayers, black phosphorus, group-IV monochalcogenides, and borophene, exhibit more intricate atomic arrangements than the hexagonal lattices of graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. The reduced symmetries of these emerging materials lead to a diverse range of electronic, optical, valley, and spin responses, including ferroelasticity, ferroelectricity, magnetism, spin-wave phenomena, large nonlinear optical properties, photogalvanic effects, and superconductivity. Additionally, these materials can exhibit novel electronic topological properties, nonlinear elastic properties, and structural phase transformations due to their low symmetry. The "Beyond Graphene: Low-Symmetry and Anisotropic 2D Materials" special topic brings together recent experimental and theoretical research on these innovative materials.

In the framework of general relativity, the Kerr spacetime stands as the sole stationary solution describing an isolated black hole, characterized by a distinctive multipolar structure determined exclusively by its mass and angular momentum. This study introduces a comprehensive methodology for computing multipole moments in generic stationary spacetimes, which we subsequently apply to an extensive class of horizonless microstate geometries. Unlike the Kerr metric, these solutions may violate both axial and equatorial symmetries, exhibiting a far more intricate multipolar structure that offers a phenomenological window into constraining fuzzball models. Our numerical analysis reveals a consistent trend: all multipole moments in these geometries generally exceed (in absolute magnitude) their Kerr black hole counterparts with equivalent mass and spin parameters. While present observational constraints on black-hole candidates' quadrupole moments impose only modest limitations on fuzzball scenarios, forthcoming gravitational-wave observations from LISA's extreme mass-ratio inspirals are projected to enhance these constraints by several orders of magnitude.

The so-called "spam king" of the internet, Scott Richter, is now seeking bankruptcy protection as anti-spam laws take their toll. Richter, who runs OptInRealBig.com and has sent billions of spam emails, claims that ongoing legal challenges have driven the company to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. One of the primary legal battles OptInRealBig is facing is with Microsoft, which is demanding millions of dollars in damages. The company believes that filing for Chapter 11 will allow it to address its legal challenges while continuing its business operations. Ranked as the world's third-largest spammer by the junk mail monitoring group Spamhaus, OptInRealBig was sued in December 2003 for distributing emails that breached anti-spam regulations. The lawsuit was initiated by Microsoft and New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer, who accuse Richter and his cohorts of sending billions of spam emails through 514 compromised internet addresses across 35 countries.

Spintronic nanodevices exhibit ultrafast, nonlinear dynamic and recurrence behaviors on a nanosecond scale, which holds great promise for enabling spintronic reservoir computing (RC) systems. In this study, we propose and model two physical RC systems based on a single magnetic skyrmion memristor (MSM) and 24 spin-torque nano-oscillators (STNOs) for image classification and nonlinear dynamic system prediction tasks. Our micromagnetic simulations reveal that a RC system utilizing a single MSM achieves outstanding performance in handwritten digit recognition. Moreover, our results demonstrate that a physical RC system consisting of 24 STNOs can effectively solve complex, nonlinear dynamic problems, including nonlinear dynamic system prediction and NARMA10 tasks.
