Brizzel to Defend 200m Title at AAA Championships in Sheffield

Paul Brizzel, the talented Irish sprinter, will be among eight of Ireland's European Indoor hopefuls competing in this weekend's AAA Championships in Sheffield. With his impressive performance in the British Trials, Brizzel is eager to defend his 200m title. The in-form athlete has been dominating the European circuit in recent weeks, and his recent ranking as the fastest 800m runner in the world this winter has solidified his position as a top contender in the Madrid championships.

Meanwhile, US-based Alistair Cragg and Mark Carroll, the only Irish athletes selected so far for the Europeans, will not compete in Sheffield. Cragg, ranked number one in the world 3000m ranking, will be focusing on his event in Madrid, while Carroll, ranked number three in the world 3000m ranking, has confirmed his participation in the European championships.

In-form James McIlroy will hope to confirm his place in the British team for Madrid by winning the 800m title in Sheffield.

Debra Hill, the renowned screenwriter and producer behind the iconic 1978 horror classic "Halloween," has passed away in Los Angeles at the age of 54, succumbing to a long battle with cancer. Hill, who co-wrote the seminal film with John Carpenter, is best known for her groundbreaking work on the groundbreaking horror classic that catapulted Jamie Lee Curtis to stardom as a babysitter terrorized by a psychopathic killer. Directed by Carpenter, the film's phenomenal success, grossing over $60 million worldwide, shattered the record for an independent film at the time, cementing its status as a horror icon. Hill's impressive body of work also includes collaborations with Carpenter on "Escape from New York" and "The Fog." Born in New Jersey, Hill began her illustrious career as a production assistant, working her way up the ranks to become an assistant director and second-unit director before partnering with Carpenter to create some of the most enduring horror classics of the 1970s. My journey to become a polar specialist, specializing in the polar regions, began at a tender age of four, when my family relocated from southern Canada to northern Baffin Island, Greenland. We settled in the tiny Inuit community of 200 Inuit residents, where we were one of only three non-Inuit families. In this isolated community, we lived without the luxuries of modern technology - no television, no computers, no radio, and no telephone. My days were filled with outdoor activities with the Inuit, playing in the snow and ice, and they became my mentors, teaching me the ways of the polar world. It was there that I developed a profound passion for this unforgiving yet breathtaking environment, and I became truly enamored with the polar realm.

The proposed approach introduces a powerful tool in multimedia forensics, leveraging the frequency fluctuations of an electric network. An efficient method for estimating network frequency fluctuations is introduced, utilizing a novel approach based on temporal windowing and the filter-bank Capon spectral estimator. The proposed method employs a type of Goerg-Semencul factorization of the model covariance matrix, which is feasible due to the Toeplitz structure of the covariance matrix. Furthermore, this approach innovatively employs a non-rectangular temporal window, rather than a traditional rectangular one, prior to spectral estimation. Krylov matrices are utilized for the efficient implementation of matrix inversions. The proposed approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in network frequency estimation, particularly when a short time window of 1 second is employed in power recordings. In speech recordings, the proposed approach yields highly accurate results in terms of both time complexity and accuracy.

Tottenham Sporting Director Frank Arnesen has firmly dismissed rumors of a personal conflict with former coach Jacques Santini being the reason for his abrupt resignation. Recent media reports had suggested Santini felt his authority was being undermined by Arnesen's influence at White Hart Lane.  

Speaking to BBC Radio Five Live, Arnesen stated, "These claims are completely unfounded. Jacques resigned solely due to private matters—it had nothing to do with any professional disagreements. He personally assured me that his decision was entirely his own."  

When pressed for details on Santini's sudden departure after only 13 matches in charge, Arnesen admitted he had no further insight. "Jacques never disclosed the specifics, and I respect his privacy. We must accept his decision with understanding and dignity," he added.

The critically acclaimed comedy *Sideways* swept this year’s Independent Spirit Awards, securing all six honors for which it was nominated. The film claimed the coveted title of Best Feature, while director Alexander Payne triumphed with dual wins for Best Director and Best Screenplay, sharing the latter accolade with co-writer Jim Taylor. The ensemble cast further elevated the film’s success, with Paul Giamatti, Thomas Haden Church, and Virginia Madsen each earning acting awards.  

With five Academy Award nominations, *Sideways* is poised for potential Oscar glory this Sunday. Celebrating its 20th anniversary, the Spirit Awards recognize outstanding achievements in cinema produced beyond the confines of major studios and are traditionally held on the eve of the Oscars.  

Among other notable winners was Catalina Sandino Moreno, honored as Best Actress for her riveting portrayal of a drug courier in the Colombian drama *Maria Full of Grace*. Moreno also carries her momentum into the Oscars, where she is nominated in the same category. Joshua Marston received the Best First Screenplay award for his work on *Maria Full of Grace*, rounding out the evening’s distinguished honorees.

Autonomous vehicle (AV) control has been shown to significantly reduce the impact of selfish routing on network efficiency. However, the assumption that AVs would readily allow themselves to be controlled externally by a central authority is unrealistic. In this paper, we propose a novel joint routing and pricing control scheme that aims to incentivize AVs to seek centrally controlled system-optimal routing by minimizing tolls while ensuring user equilibrium and human-driven vehicles are subject to a congestion charge. The problem is formulated as a bilevel optimization problem, where dynamic tolls are optimized in the upper level, whereas the lower level is a mixed equilibrium simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model considering a mixed fleet of AVs and human-driven vehicles.

As I reflect on the journey that has unfolded over the past eight years, I am reminded of the moment when I first took the stage at TED, fresh out of graduate school. It was a pivotal moment that not only shaped the trajectory of my career but also framed the questions that I would be asked for the next decade. Like many others in my generation, I was eager to tackle the world's most pressing issues, particularly the complex and multifaceted problem of water quality. However, I was naive in my optimism, believing that the root of the issue lay in the complexity of the problem itself. I thought that all we needed was simpler, faster, and more affordable sensors to detect the issues. I was wrong. The reality is that the challenges we face in addressing water quality are far more profound and interconnected than I initially realized, and it has taken time, experience, and a willingness to listen and learn to truly make a meaningful impact.

Sustainability concerns itself with the specifics of what is caught, where it is caught, and how it is caught. However, I am more interested in understanding the people involved—their motivations and their stories. I care about the individuals who contribute to my dining experience and how my choices affect their lives and vice versa. I want to learn why they fish and how they depend on the ocean's resources for their livelihoods. By gaining this understanding, we can transform our view of seafood from just a consumable product to an opportunity to rejuvenate our ecosystems. It allows us to appreciate the seafood we are lucky enough to consume. So, what do we call this approach? I believe it is best described as "restorative seafood." While sustainability focuses on enduring and maintaining, restoration emphasizes replenishing and advancing.

New research suggests that lowering the drink-drive limit to 50mg of alcohol per 100ml of blood could save more lives than previously thought. According to a report from University College London, cutting the limit could save 65 lives a year, compared to the previous estimate of 50 lives. The study, conducted by transport expert Professor Richard Allsop, also found that the move would lead to 230 fewer serious injuries on the UK's roads, resulting in cost savings of £119m per year. The findings are being published as MPs prepare to vote on the government's Road Safety Bill, which includes changes to speeding fines but not the drink-drive limit. The research suggests that a lower drink-drive limit could have a significant impact on road safety in the UK.

When a new user just signs up on a website, we usually have no information about him/her, i.e. No interaction with items, no user profile and no social links with other users. Under such circumstances, we still expect our recommender systems could attract the users at the first time so that the users decide to stay on the website and become active users. This problem falls into new user cold-start category and it is crucial to the development and even survival of a company. Existing works on user cold-start recommendation either require additional user efforts, e.g. Setting up an interview process, or make use of side information [10] such as user demographics, locations, social relations, etc. However, users may not be willing to take the interview and side information on cold-start users is usually not available.

Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in josephson junctions offers an intriguing way to combine anomalous hall and josephson physics in a single device. We study theoretically how the superposition of both effects impacts superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions' transport properties. Transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of cooper pairs through the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface creates sizable transverse hall supercurrents, to which we refer as anomalous josephson hall effect currents. We generalize the furusaki-tsukada formula, which got initially established to quantify usual (tunneling) josephson current flows, to evaluate the transverse current components and demonstrate that their amplitudes are widely adjustable by means of the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. As a clear spectroscopic fingerprint of josephson junctions, well-localized subgap bound states form around the interface.

The history of the Milky Way's assembly is currently being unveiled thanks to the data provided by the Gaia mission. It is likely that the globular cluster system of our galaxy has followed a similarly intricate formation path. To constrain this formation path, we explore the link between the globular clusters and the known merging events that the Milky Way has experienced. To achieve this, we combined the kinematic information provided by Gaia for almost all galactic clusters, with the largest sample of cluster ages available after carefully correcting for systematic errors. To identify clusters with a common origin, we analyzed their dynamical properties, particularly in the space of integrals of motion. Our results show that about 40% of the clusters likely formed in situ.

The paper proposes a new framework called Temporal Graph Neural Networks (TGNN) that leverages temporal point processes and variational autoencoders to learn temporal attention between nodes in a graph. The proposed framework aims to alleviate issues associated with human-specified edges, which can be both expensive to produce and suboptimal for downstream tasks.
 The key idea is to learn temporal attention between nodes by observing node interactions over time. This is achieved by using temporal point processes to model the dynamics of node interactions and variational autoencoders to infer temporal attention between nodes.
The proposed framework consists of two main components:
1. Temporal Point Processes (TPPs): TPPs are used to model the dynamics of node interactions over time. TPPs are based on the idea of a point process, where each node in the graph is represented by a point in a high-dimensional space. The position of each point in this space captures the state of the node at a given time step.
2. Variational Autoencoders (VAEs): VAEs are used to infer temporal attention between nodes. VAEs are based on the idea of variational inference, which is a method for approximating complex probability distributions. In the context of TGNNs, VAEs are

The tails of diboson production at the lhc are sensitive to the interference between standard model and higher dimension operators parameterizing the effects of heavy new physics. However, helicity selection rules for the diboson scattering amplitudes set an obstruction to the na\"ive interference contributions of dimension six operators, causing the total diboson rate correction's leading contribution to cancel. In this case, carefully measuring the azimuthal decay angles "resurrects" the interference, recouping sensitivity to the "non-interfering" operators. We explore these signatures in detail, and find that the eft uncertainties associated with higher-dimensional operators are uniquely well-suppressed by the construction of an asymmetry variable which is only generated by these non-interfering operators, relegating the effects of higher-dimensional, interfering operators to the same status as statistical errors in this observable.

We study the transport properties and superconducting proximity effect in nsn junctions formed by a time-reversal symmetry broken weyl semimetal (wsm) in proximity to an $s$-wave superconductor. We find that the differential conductance and induced pairing amplitudes strongly depend on the angle between the junction direction in real space and the axis separating the weyl nodes in momentum space. We identify the influence of a chiral chemical potential, i.e., the electron population imbalance between weyl nodes of opposite chirality, on the transport characteristics of the junction. Remarkably, we observe a net spin polarization of cooper pairs that are generated via andreev reflection in the two wsm regions. The spin polarization is opposite in the two wsm regions and highly sensitive to the chirality imbalance and excitation energy.

It was the fall of 1974, and I found myself mired in a desperate struggle to comprehend the second chapter of my high school algebra textbook. As someone who has never been inclined towards mathematics, I was facing an uphill battle. My aversion to math was, and still is, profound. The mere thought of it fills me with anxiety. As I wrestled with the abstract concepts, the doorbell pierced the air, shattering my concentration. I set aside my book and made my way to the entrance, only to be met with an empty porch. The disappointment was palpable. I returned to my studies, and just as a glimmer of understanding began to emerge from the depths of my subconscious, the doorbell rang once more. The fleeting moment of clarity vanished instantly. I rose from my seat, only to be greeted by the same eerie silence. It was as if some mischievous prankster had taken it upon themselves to disrupt my already fragile grasp on the subject, leaving me to wonder if I would ever find solace in the world of mathematics.

Our primary objective was to identify any contractual language that might obligate us to perform a true-up for the customer, regardless of whether we allocated storage on a specific gas day. Interfacial spin-orbit coupling in Josephson junctions presents a groundbreaking opportunity to harmoniously integrate anomalous Hall and Josephson physics within a single device. We theoretically investigate how the simultaneous presence of both effects influences the transport properties of superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator/superconductor junctions. The transverse momentum-dependent skew tunneling of Cooper pairs through the spin-active ferromagnetic insulator interface generates substantial transverse Hall supercurrents, which we refer to as anomalous Josephson Hall effect currents. We extend the Furusaki-Tsukada formula, initially developed to quantify traditional Josephson current flows, to evaluate the transverse current components and demonstrate that their amplitudes are readily adjustable via the spin-orbit coupling strengths or the superconducting phase difference across the junction. As a distinctive spectroscopic hallmark of Josephson junctions, well-localized subgap bound states form around the interface, providing a clear signature of the junction's functionality.

He is trying to make the case that using place as the center of culture gives a more grounded community. I am a connoisseur of culture in all its forms. My daily commute is soundtracked by an eclectic playlist—from the grandeur of Wagner and Mozart to the pop brilliance of Christina Aguilera, the soulful twang of Josh Turner, and the raw energy of Kirk Franklin’s gospel rap. Symphonies, concertos, and an ever-expanding library of music accompany me everywhere.  

My literary appetite is equally insatiable. I traverse the spectrum from Ian McEwan’s profound narratives to Stephanie Meyer’s addictive sagas—yes, I’ve journeyed through the entire *Twilight* series.  

At home, my sanctuary is my home theater, where I indulge in a feast of visual storytelling: DVDs, on-demand films, and television series. Whether it’s the gripping drama of *Law & Order: SVU*, the razor-sharp wit of Tina Fey in *30 Rock*, or the unfiltered justice of *Judge Judy*—where real cases meet final rulings—I immerse myself in it all.

Deep learning has revolutionized the field of problem-solving, tackling complex challenges that were previously insurmountable for heuristic algorithms. Notably, its successful application in wireless communications has yielded significant benefits, even in areas where existing radio systems are well-established and optimal algorithms are readily available. While learning individual components of a receiver has led to some improvements, a more effective approach involves jointly learning the entire receiver pipeline. However, this holistic approach often gives rise to a formidable nonlinear problem, whose optimal solution is impractical to implement. To address this challenge, we introduce Deeprx, a novel deep fully convolutional neural network that seamlessly integrates the entire receiver pipeline, from frequency domain signal processing to uncoded bit extraction, in a manner compliant with 5G standards. By carefully crafting the input to the convolutional neural network using a combination of data and pilot symbols, we enable accurate channel estimation, thereby enhancing the overall performance of the receiver.

Introducing a Novel Mixed Variable Symplectic Integrator for Planetary Systems: Enhanced Stability and Efficiency in Close Encounter Simulations

A groundbreaking mixed variable symplectic (MVS) integrator has been developed to accurately model planetary systems, particularly those involving close encounters. By leveraging a time regularisation technique, this innovative method successfully maintains the stability properties inherent to symplectic integrators while adaptively reducing the effective step size during planetary interactions. This approach enables the utilisation of high-order MVS schemes, facilitating large time steps in regions far from close encounters. Notably, the algorithm demonstrates exceptional performance in resolving near-collision events, characterised by mutual separations of mere fractions of the physical radius, without compromising the time-step size typically employed in weakly perturbed systems, such as our solar system. The long-term behaviour of the integrator is showcased through simulations of six super-Earths undergoing intense scattering over a period of 50,000 years. Comparative analyses with hybrid methods, including the Mercury algorithm, reveal that the proposed MVS integrator achieves superior energy conservation at equivalent computational costs.

Last night in class, we discussed the assigned reading, "Urbanization and the Meaning of Place" by Edward S. Casey. The first chapter focuses on the concept of "implacement" and the second, "displacement." Casey argues that our contemporary world and culture are centered around time, as we are constantly running out of time, just in time, and trying to make the most of it. He posits that shifting the focus to place as the central organizing principle can lead to a more grounded community. So far, this book has been particularly captivating, as it aligns with my own thoughts on the subject.

Reducing the legal drink-driving limit could save more lives than previously estimated, according to a new report. Research from University College London suggests that lowering the limit from 80mg to 50mg of alcohol per 100ml of blood could prevent 65 deaths annually. These findings are being released by the Parliamentary Advisory Council for Transport Safety as MPs prepare to vote on the government's road safety bill, which addresses speeding fines but not the drink-driving limit. Conducted by transport expert Professor Richard Allsop, the research also indicates that this change could result in 230 fewer serious injuries on UK roads compared to 2003, potentially saving the country £119 million each year. A prior estimate from a 1998 Department of Transport report projected that such a reduction might save 50 lives annually.

Novel concepts, perspectives and challenges in measuring and controlling an open quantum system via sequential schemes are shown. We discuss how similar protocols, relying both on repeated quantum measurements and dynamical decoupling control pulses, can allow to: (i) confine and protect quantum dynamics from decoherence in accordance with the zeno physics. (ii) analytically predict the probability that a quantum system is transferred into a target quantum state by means of stochastic sequential measurements. (iii) optimally reconstruct the spectral density of environmental noise sources by orthogonalizing in the frequency domain the filter functions driving the designed quantum-sensor. The achievement of these tasks will enhance our capability to observe and manipulate open quantum systems, thus bringing advances to quantum science and technologies.

Weight pruning has been widely recognized as a simple and effective method to eliminate redundancy in deep neural networks (DNNs), resulting in accelerated performance on various platforms. However, most pruning techniques are trade-offs between model accuracy and regularity, leading to compromised inference accuracy and limited on-device acceleration. To address this issue, we introduce a novel sparsity dimension, pattern-based sparsity, which seamlessly integrates pattern and connectivity sparsity. Our approach achieves unprecedented accuracy enhancement and improved feature extraction capabilities across various DNN architectures and datasets, while also enabling efficient optimization for mobile platforms through our pattern-aware pruning framework. This framework enables pattern library extraction, pattern selection, pattern and connectivity pruning, and weight training to be performed simultaneously, resulting in a highly optimized and efficient DNN execution. Our innovative use of pattern-based sparsity naturally lends itself to efficient compiler optimization for mobile platforms, leading to unparalleled performance gains.

**Redefining Parenthood in the Modern Age**  

The essence of parenthood is far from simple. In today’s world, families take many forms—adoptive homes, blended families, surrogacy arrangements—each presenting unique challenges and profound questions. How should parents navigate conversations about sperm donation? When is the right time to broach the subject, and what language best honors the child’s understanding? Terms like "biological father" spark debate: does "father" truly capture the nuance of such relationships?  

As a philosopher and social scientist, I’ve dedicated years to examining these complexities. But beyond theory, the most illuminating insights come from the voices of parents and children themselves. Their experiences reveal a universal truth: what truly binds a family isn’t its structure, but the love and commitment at its core. Today, I’ll share their wisdom—proof that even unconventional families understand what matters most.

At Casa Pescatore, my greatest joy has always been ensuring every guest feels as comfortable and welcome as they would in their own home. Yesterday, I believe I achieved that. The neighborhood gathered on Perry Avenue with an enthusiasm some might call overwhelming. At 4:30 PM, my dearest friend Eric and his son Nunu arrived, bearing a massive pan of baked ziti. The tables were meticulously arranged, laden with my homemade dishes, while laughter and chatter filled the air like joyous bells.  

Then, in a moment that warmed my heart, my closest friend from Florida walked through the door—a reunion I’d longed for. His presence alone was a gift, but the delightful surprise was his wife joining him. With her expecting a child, this is the nearest I’ll come to unclehood, and I embraced the role wholeheartedly. I showered her with endless trays of food and tiramisu, savoring every second of their company.

In the framework of general relativity, the Kerr spacetime stands as the singular stationary solution for an isolated black hole, characterized by a distinctive multipolar structure that is solely determined by its mass and spin. This work introduces a novel methodology for extracting multipole moments from arbitrary stationary spacetimes, which is subsequently applied to a vast array of horizonless microstate geometries. Notably, these geometries can disrupt the axial and equatorial symmetries inherent to the Kerr metric, thereby exhibiting a far more intricate multipolar structure. This, in turn, offers a unique opportunity to phenomenologically constrain fuzzball models. Our analysis reveals compelling numerical evidence suggesting that the multipole moments of these geometries are generally more pronounced than those of a Kerr black hole with equivalent mass and spin. Presently, measurements of the quadrupole moment of black-hole candidates impose only modest constraints on fuzzball models. However, forthcoming gravitational-wave detections of extreme mass-ratio inspirals via the LISA space mission are poised to significantly enhance these bounds, yielding improvements of several orders of magnitude.

Captains lining up for aid match ireland's brian o'driscoll is one of four six nations captains included in the northern hemisphere squad for the irb rugby aid match on 5 march. We have developed single-shot shaped pulses designed for ultra-high fidelity (uh-fidelity) population transfer within a three-level quantum system configured in a lambda arrangement. To achieve high fidelity, we employ the Lewis-Riesenfeld (L-R) method to derive a family of solutions that facilitate exact transfer, ensuring that these solutions adhere to a single dynamical mode of the L-R invariant. From this family, we identify a tracking solution characterized by a single parameter, which effectively controls the fidelity of the transfer, the population within the excited state, and the robustness of the system. We define the robustness of an uh-fidelity transfer as the minimum percentile deviation in pulse areas that results in infidelity exceeding $10^{-4}$. Our shaped pulses demonstrate superior robustness compared to Gaussian and adiabatically-optimized pulses when considering moderate pulse areas.

Deep learning has made significant strides in solving problems that were previously out of reach of heuristic algorithms. It has also been successfully applied to wireless communication systems, even in areas where well-established radio systems are already optimized and optimal algorithms exist for many tasks. While some advancements have been achieved by learning individual components of a receiver, a more effective approach is to jointly learn the entire receiver. However, this often results in a challenging non-linear problem, for which the optimal solution is difficult to implement. To address this challenge, we propose a deep fully convolutional neural network, deeprx, which processes the entire receiver pipeline from frequency domain signal stream to uncoded bits in a 5G-compliant manner. By constructing the input of the convolutional neural network in a very specific manner using both the data and pilot symbols, we facilitate accurate channel estimation and ensure the efficient processing of the receiver.

In this work, a generalization of the study of the human gait was made from already existent models in the literature, like models of keller and kockshenev. In this hybrid model, a strategy of metabolic energy minimization is combined in a race process, with a non-linear description of the movement of the mass center's libration, trying to reproduce the behavior of the walk-run transition. The results of the experimental data, for different speed regimes, indicate that the perimeter of the trajectory of the mass center is a relevant quantity in the quantification of this dynamic. An experimental procedure was put into practice in collaboration with the research group in biomedical engineering, basic sciences and laboratories of the manuela beltr\'an university in bogot\'a, colombia.

In 1903, the 26th President of the United States, Theodore Roosevelt, embarked on a three-day camping excursion in California's breathtaking Yosemite Valley. Roosevelt slept among the towering sequoia groves, braved a snowstorm, and spent countless hours engaging in spirited conversations around the campfire with his host and guide, the renowned conservationist John Muir. Roosevelt, an ardent lover of nature, was invited by Muir for more than just a camping trip - Yosemite was in peril. Although the valley was protected land since 1864, it remained vulnerable to overdevelopment in 1903, at the center of a decades-old struggle to strike a balance between preservation and public use. The contentious battle over Yosemite began in 1849 with the Gold Rush, as miners flocked west in search of gold in the Sierra Nevada foothills.

Baryonic feedback effects lead to a suppression of the weak lensing angular power spectrum on small scales. The poorly constrained shape and amplitude of this suppression is an important source of uncertainties for upcoming cosmological weak lensing surveys such as euclid or lsst. In this first paper in a series of two, we use simulations to build a euclid-like tomographic mock data-set for the cosmic shear power spectrum and the corresponding covariance matrix, which are both corrected for baryonic effects following the baryonification method of schneider et al. (2019). In addition, we develop an emulator to obtain fast predictions of the baryonic power suppression, allowing us to perform a likelihood inference analysis for a standard $\lambda$cdm cosmology with both cosmological and astrophysical parameters.

Podcasting has revolutionized the way people consume audio content, allowing users to store up to 10,000 songs on their Apple iPods or other digital music players. However, this vast storage capacity has not been filled to its maximum potential. Many iPod owners are increasingly opting to fill this space with a diverse range of audio content, courtesy of an eclectic mix of creators. The concept of podcasting, championed by its most ardent advocate, former MTV host and VJ Adam Curry, has its roots in the term "podcasting," which was inspired by the Apple iPod. Notably, one does not require an iPod to create or listen to a podcast. A podcast is essentially an internet-based radio show, typically produced from the comfort of one's own home. To get started, all one needs is a microphone, a computer, and some editing software. Podcasters then upload their shows to the internet, making them accessible for free download and listening by others.

When inquiring which aspect of psychology people find most challenging, if you pose the question, "What about cognition and emotions?" the typical response reveals a fascinating pattern. Most individuals will assert, "Emotions are profoundly difficult—they're bewilderingly complex. I can't begin to comprehend their mechanisms. But reasoning? That seems perfectly straightforward—just logical processing, really. That part isn't problematic."

This perception leads us to several critical issues worth examining. Consider, for instance, the pressing matter of public health. Recently, I encountered a striking claim suggesting that in Western societies, handshaking might represent the single most significant vector for disease transmission.

Levy takes whitbread novel prize orange prize winner andrea levy has seen her book small island win the whitbread novel of the year award. She is now favourite to win the overall prize after beating booker winner alan hollinghurst's the line of beauty. Irish sprinter Paul Brizzel will be one of eight hopefuls from Ireland competing in this weekend's European Indoor Championships in Sheffield. Alongside him, US-based Alistair Cragg and Mark Carroll will be the only Irish athletes representing their country in the event. Brizzel, who will be defending his 200m title, is expected to put in a strong performance in the British trials. Meanwhile, James McIlroy is looking to confirm his place in the British team for the Europeans by winning the 800m title. McIlroy has been in top form on the European circuit in recent weeks, and his impressive performances have secured him a spot in the Madrid-bound team. Mark Carroll, who is currently ranked third in the world 3000m ranking, has confirmed that he will join Cragg in the European Championships. The duo's participation in the event is expected to be a highlight of the competition.

In this paper we investigate multi-agent discrete-event systems with partial observation. The agents can be divided into several groups in each of which the agents have similar (isomorphic) state transition structures, and thus can be relabeled into the same template. Based on the template a scalable supervisor whose state size and computational cost are independent of the number of agents is designed for the case of partial observation. The scalable supervisor under partial observation does not need to be recomputed regardless of how many agents are added to or removed from the system. We generalize our earlier results to partial observation by proposing sufficient conditions for safety and maximal permissiveness of the scalable least restrictive supervisor on the template level. An example is provided to illustrate the proposed scalable supervisory synthesis.

Building upon insights from Monte Carlo simulations (Jiménez, J. Turbul. 2020), which revealed dipole structures to be as dynamically significant as individual vortex cores in decaying two-dimensional turbulence, this study demonstrates that the system's kinetic energy is predominantly channeled through extended streams composed of interconnected dipoles. The vortex population segregates into two distinct classes: a collection of compact, rapidly propagating cores and an ensemble of larger, quasi-stationary structures that appear effectively "frozen" within a gradually evolving crystalline framework. These two vortex populations exhibit markedly different kinematic behaviors, with only the smaller cores displaying self-similar characteristics. The larger, slow-moving vortices—which constitute the primary energy reservoir—organize into dipole configurations that coalesce to form the energetic streams. The paper further examines the underlying processes driving the development of this dominant slow component within the turbulent system.

**Subject:** Urgent Concern Regarding PX Day-Ahead Auction Disruptions Due to ISO Rule Changes  

**Date:** Monday, December 11, 2000  
**To:** All PX Market Participants  
**From:** John Yurkanin, Chief Operating Officer  

Dear Market Participants,  

The California ISO's emergency tariff filing last Friday has significantly disrupted the PX Day-Ahead Auction market. The simultaneous implementation of soft and hard caps has created a fundamental disconnect, exposing all participants to unacceptable risks. Furthermore, these changes are unlikely to achieve the ISO's intended reduction in real-time energy sales and procurement.  

As a result, supply resources will continue prioritizing the ISO's higher-priced real-time opportunities. To address this issue, the PX is formally requesting that Terry Winter of the ISO authorize immediate adjustment bids for all submitted schedules at market-relevant prices.  

We appreciate your attention to this critical matter and will keep you informed of further developments.  

Best regards,  
John Yurkanin  
Chief Operating Officer

A comprehensive investigation into the orientational dynamics of a comb-shaped nematic polymer, featuring both mesogenic and functional side groups, was conducted in its isotropic phase using a combination of Kerr effect and dielectric spectroscopy techniques. Notably, this study revealed a significant departure from the behavior of low-molecular-weight mesogens, as the relaxation of electric birefringence in the polymer melt above the nematic-isotropic phase transition temperature could be accurately described by a multi-exponential decay process, with two dominant relaxation mechanisms playing a crucial role. These primary processes exhibit a temperature-dependent crossover, occurring within a range of approximately 50 degrees. Furthermore, dielectric spectroscopy enabled the identification of two distinct orientational relaxation processes: one attributed to the rotational motion of the side mesogenic groups, and the other associated with the movement of main chain segments.

Us woman sues over cartridges a us woman is suing hewlett packard (hp), saying its printer ink cartridges are secretly programmed to expire on a certain date. The unnamed woman from georgia says that a chip inside the cartridge tells the printer that it needs re-filling even when it does not. The lawsuit seeks to represent anyone in the us who has purchased an hp inkjet printer since february 2001. Hp, the world's biggest printer firm, declined to comment on the lawsuit. Hp ink cartridges use a chip technology to sense when they are low on ink and advise the user to make a change. But the suit claims the chips also shut down the cartridges at a predetermined date regardless of whether they are empty.

Melzer shocks agassi second seed andre agassi suffered a comprehensive defeat by jurgen melzer in the quarter-finals of the sap open. Agassi was often bamboozled by the austrian's drop shots in san jose, losing 6-3 6-1. Defending champion and top seed andy roddick rallied to beat sweden's thomas enqvist 3-6 7-6 (8-6) 7-5. But unseeded cyril saulnier beat the fourth seed vincent spadea 6-2 6-4 and tommy haas overcame eighth seed max mirnyi 6-7 (2-7) 7-6 (7-3) 6-2. This weekend, the AAA's Championships in Sheffield will play host to eight of Ireland's top athletes vying for a spot in the European Indoor Championships, including Ballymena sprinter Paul Brizzel. Notably absent from the competition will be US-based Alistair Cragg and Mark Carroll, both of whom have already secured their places in the European Championships. Brizzel will be looking to retain his 200m title at the British trials, while in-form James McIlroy will be seeking to cement his position in the British team for Madrid by claiming the 800m title. McIlroy has been in exceptional form on the European circuit in recent weeks, establishing himself as one of the fastest 800m runners in the world this winter and making a strong case for his inclusion in the Madrid lineup. Meanwhile, Cork's Mark Carroll has confirmed that he will join Cragg at the European Championships, with Carroll currently ranked third in the world 3000m rankings and Cragg occupying the top spot.

The weak gravity conjecture (wgc) demands the existence of superextremal particles in any consistent quantum theory of gravity. The standard lore is that these particles are introduced to ensure that extremal black holes are either unstable or marginally stable, but it is not clear what is wrong if this doesn't happen. This note shows that, for a generic einstein quantum theory of gravity in ads, exactly stability of extremal black branes is in tension with rigorously proven quantum information theorems about entanglement entropy. Avoiding the contradiction leads to a nonperturbative version of the wgc, which reduces to the usual statement at weak coupling. The argument is general, and it does not rely on either supersymmetry or a particular uv completion, assuming only the validity of einsteinian gravity, effective field theory, and holography.

While I have no qualms about assisting others with their technology, I cannot help but wonder at the frequency with which I find myself in this situation. In the past few weeks, I've been sacrificing my own rest to ensure that others' computers are in working order, and I must admit that I'm starting to feel a bit taken advantage of. Despite my exhaustion, I find myself watching Joe Schmo 2 tonight, rather than retiring to bed early. Sid Meier's Civilization: Beyond Earth is a turn-based strategy game where your objective is to develop your civilization to achieve specific goals or 'victories'. Unlike the previous installments, which unfolded against Earth's historical timeline—covering eras like the Stone Age, Medieval Age, and the Modern Age—this game is set in a futuristic scenario where your mission is to colonize a new planet. What I particularly appreciate about this game is that 2K Games took a fresh approach by implementing innovative mechanics such as the affinity system and the orbital layer. However, I found that these new mechanics could sometimes make the game feel a bit overwhelming. An area where I believe the game could be enhanced is by incorporating new artwork, particularly in the environments you encounter during interactions with other leaders.

Attention Magic enthusiasts: Be cautious, as Wizards of the Coast appears reluctant to create a satisfying digital Magic: The Gathering experience. At its essence, the game is a social outlet crafted for camaraderie and competition—sharing laughs, playful banter, and triumphant victories with friends or friendly rivals. It seems that Wizards of the Coast has lost touch with this spirit. In the digital platform, playing with friends yields no gold, which is vital for progression in the game. Gold is so crucial that there's a daily earning limit, which makes sense, given that gold is the key to unlocking everything within the game. However, brace yourself for the tedious grind of 1v1 battles against either the computer or random opponents—with whom you can't communicate. Choosing the free-to-play path means enduring roughly four hours of solo play each day for over a month to gather the necessary cards.

We establish natural splittings for the values of global mackey functors at orthogonal, unitary and symplectic groups. In particular, the restriction homomorphisms between the orthogonal, unitary and symplectic groups of adjacent dimensions are naturally split epimorphisms. The interest in the splitting comes from equivariant stable homotopy theory. The equivariant stable homotopy groups of every global spectrum form a global mackey functor, so the splittings imply that certain long exact homotopy group sequences separate into short exact sequences. For the real and complex global thom spectra $\mathbf{mo}$ and $\mathbf{mu}$, the splittings imply the regularity of various euler classes related to the tautological representations of $o(n)$ and $u(n)$.

The global demand for life-saving organ transplants far exceeds the available supply, leaving hundreds of thousands of patients in desperate need of kidneys, hearts, and livers. This critical shortage has spurred groundbreaking innovations in regenerative medicine, particularly in the emerging field of bioprinting. Imagine a future where we could engineer fully functional, personalized organs rather than relying solely on donor transplants. While we haven't yet perfected the creation of complex organs, scientists have already achieved remarkable success in bioprinting fundamental biological structures like blood vessels and tubular networks essential for nutrient delivery and waste removal. This revolutionary technology builds upon the principles of 3D printing, meticulously assembling living tissues layer by layer to construct viable biological structures with unprecedented precision.

Deep learning has revolutionized problem-solving by tackling challenges beyond the scope of traditional heuristic approaches. Its remarkable success extends to wireless communications, despite the field's well-established theoretical foundations and existing optimal algorithms for numerous functions. While incremental improvements have been achieved by optimizing isolated receiver components, superior performance emerges when the entire receiver system is learned holistically. This comprehensive approach, however, presents a complex nonlinear optimization problem where ideal solutions remain computationally impractical. Addressing this challenge, we introduce DeepRx – an innovative fully convolutional neural network architecture that seamlessly processes the complete receiver chain from frequency-domain signal streams to decoded bits while maintaining full 5G compliance. Our novel input construction methodology, which strategically combines both data and pilot symbols, enables exceptionally precise channel estimation within the convolutional network framework.

When constructing models to summarize clinical data to be used for simulations, it is good practice to evaluate the models for their capacity to reproduce the data. This can be done by means of visual predictive checks (vpc), which consist of (1) several reproductions of the original study by simulation from the model under evaluation, (2) calculating estimates of interest for each simulated study and (3) comparing the distribution of those estimates with the estimate from the original study. This procedure is a generic method that is straightforward to apply, in general. Here we consider the application of the method to time to event data and consider the special case when a time-varying covariate is not known or cannot be approximated after event time.

We introduce a systematic framework for decomposing the values of global Mackey functors associated with orthogonal, unitary, and symplectic groups. Notably, the restriction homomorphisms between these groups of consecutive dimensions admit natural splittings, yielding epimorphisms that are inherently split. This decomposition is of particular interest in the context of equivariant stable homotopy theory, where the equivariant stable homotopy groups of any global spectrum collectively form a global Mackey functor. Consequently, our splittings enable the disentanglement of intricate long exact homotopy group sequences into more manageable short exact sequences. Furthermore, when applied to the real and complex global Thom spectra, namely $\mathbf{mo}$ and $\mathbf{mu}$, these splittings reveal the regularity of various Euler classes that are intimately connected to the tautological representations of the orthogonal and unitary groups $o(n)$ and $u(n)$, respectively.

The estimation of the intrinsic dimension of a dataset is a fundamental step in most dimensionality reduction techniques. This article illustrates intrinsic, an r package that implements novel state-of-the-art likelihood-based estimators of the intrinsic dimension of a dataset. In detail, the methods included in this package are the two-nn, gride, and hidalgo models. To allow these novel estimators to be easily accessible, the package contains a few high-level, intuitive functions that rely on a broader set of efficient, low-level routines. Intrinsic encompasses models that fall into two categories: homogeneous and heterogeneous intrinsic dimension estimators. The first category contains the two-nn and gride models. The functions dedicated to these two methods carry out inference under both the frequentist and bayesian frameworks. In the second category we find hidalgo, a bayesian mixture model, for which an efficient gibbs sampler is implemented.

World leaders gather to face uncertainty more than 2,000 business and political leaders from around the globe are arriving in the swiss mountain resort davos for the annual world economic forum (wef). For five days, they will discuss issues ranging from china's economic power to iraq's future after this sunday's elections. Uk prime minister tony blair and south african president thabo mbeki are among the more than 20 government leaders and heads of state leaders attending the meeting. Unlike previous years, protests against the wef are expected to be muted. Anti-globalisation campaigners have called off a demonstration planned for the weekend. The brazilian city of porto alegre will host the rival world social forum, timed to run in parallel with the wef's ritzier event in davos.

As the UK prepares for a highly anticipated general election, the nation's economic future is poised to take centre stage in Labour's campaign, according to Chancellor Gordon Brown. This assertion comes on the heels of the final cabinet meeting at 10 Downing Street before the expected election announcement. Brown expressed confidence that voters will acknowledge Labour's track record of fostering stability and growth, and that the party will continue to build upon this momentum. In a parallel development, the Conservative Party unveiled its strategy to combat anti-social behaviour, while the Liberal Democrats provided further insight into their proposals for replacing council tax. In a timely intervention, the Archbishop of Canterbury penned an open letter to the three main parties, cautioning against exploiting the electorate's fears during the campaign. He urged politicians to refrain from transforming the election into a competition centred on who can most effectively instil fear in voters regarding terrorism, asylum, and crime.

Gop gets scolded on recruitment naturalization ceremonies in jacksonville are to be relocated after complaints that gop activists registered new citizens as republicans. By gary fineout and alfonso chardy the citizenship ceremony in jacksonville seemed to go off as usual, with a crowd of nearly 200 people going home as new americans. Just before the new citizens left the june 29 event, an immigration official directing the swearing-in urged them to stop by a voter registration table -- a not uncommon sight at naturalization ceremonies. But this table was unusual: those handing out forms were republican volunteers -- and the party affiliation box had been checked off ahead of time to make all of the new voters members of the gop. All of it was suspicious to linda cross, who was there to watch her husband, dario cruz, take his citizenship oath.

The tails of diboson production at the LHC are sensitive to the interference between standard model and higher-dimensional operators, which parameterize the effects of heavy new physics. However, helicity selection rules for diboson scattering amplitudes impose an obstruction to naïve interference contributions from dimension-six operators, resulting in the leading contribution to the total diboson rate correction cancelling. In this case, measuring the azimuthal decay angles 'resurrects' the interference, recouping sensitivity to the 'non-interfering' operators. We explore these signatures in detail and find that the EFT uncertainties associated with higher-dimensional operators are uniquely well-suppressed by the construction of an asymmetry variable generated exclusively by these non-interfering operators, relegating the effects of higher-dimensional, interfering operators to the same status as statistical errors in this observable.

Republican Party Faces Criticism Over Voter Registration at Naturalization Ceremonies

Naturalization ceremonies in Jacksonville will be relocated due to concerns following allegations that Republican activists were registering new citizens as members of their party. Reported by Gary Fineout and Alfonso Chardy, the citizenship ceremony initially proceeded as expected, with nearly 200 attendees taking part and becoming new Americans. Just before leaving the event on June 29, an immigration official encouraged these new citizens to visit a voter registration table—an ordinary feature at such ceremonies. However, this table was different: Republican volunteers were distributing the forms, and the party affiliation box had already been pre-checked to register each new voter as a Republican. This appeared suspicious to Linda Cross, who was present to witness her husband, Dario Cruz, take his citizenship oath.

The widespread adoption of broadband internet is revolutionizing the way people express themselves online, with a significant proportion of users in the UK leveraging fast web access to share their thoughts, ideas, and creativity with the world. According to a comprehensive report by the UK-based think-tank Demos, nearly a quarter of broadband subscribers in Britain regularly upload content and maintain personal websites, underscoring the profound impact of high-speed internet on online behavior. The always-on, fast connection afforded by broadband is transforming the internet experience, enabling users to engage with online content in new and innovative ways. With over five million households in the UK already connected to broadband and this number growing rapidly, the report's findings offer a fascinating glimpse into the evolving online habits of the British public. Notably, the study reveals that more than half of broadband users begin their day by logging onto the web, while a significant minority even confess to rising in the middle of the night to browse the internet. Perhaps most importantly, the report argues that broadband is empowering individuals to take a more active and participatory role in the online community, fostering a culture of creativity, self-expression, and digital engagement.

Our research explores the phenomenology of a hypercharge-zero SU(2) triplet scalar field, which arises naturally in models featuring a two-stage electroweak symmetry-breaking mechanism. We analyze two distinct scenarios: one where these triplet scalars remain stable and potentially constitute a component of dark matter, and another where they decay through interactions with the Standard Model Higgs boson.  

In the stable triplet scenario, stringent constraints emerge from LHC searches for disappearing charged tracks and from direct dark matter detection experiments. For the decaying triplet case, existing multilepton collider searches impose significant limits. Our findings demonstrate that a two-step electroweak phase transition involving a stable triplet with a negative quadratic term is conclusively excluded by direct detection bounds. Furthermore, we establish that an unstable triplet with a mass below 230 GeV is ruled out at 95% confidence.

Processing spontaneous conversations in real-world environments, like those found in child-centered recordings, is recognized as one of the most complex audio challenges. Successfully developing speech processing models capable of handling such a diverse range of conditions would greatly benefit language acquisition research, which focuses on the quantity and quality of speech children are exposed to and produce. Additionally, these models would be invaluable for early diagnosis and evaluating the impact of interventions. In this paper, we introduce our methodology for creating an open-source neural network designed to categorize audio segments into vocalizations by the child wearing the recording device, vocalizations from other children, adult male speech, and adult female speech. To achieve this, we compiled a diverse set of child-centered corpora, amassing a total of 260 hours of recordings spanning 10 languages.

For the last 24 years, I have served as a firefighter in Huntington, West Virginia. My team and I are dedicated to saving lives and protecting property from various emergencies, including car accidents, house fires, and critical medical situations. As a woman leading a department in a predominantly male field, I decided to enhance my medical expertise by earning a nursing degree ten years ago. This decision was motivated by the realization that the emerging threat to my city, and others across the nation, was no longer a single, manageable disaster. Unlike before, when a firefighter could respond, extinguish the blaze, and leave with a sense of accomplishment, the challenges we face now require a more comprehensive approach to truly make a difference.

Debra Hill, the screenwriter and producer best known for her role in creating the 1970s horror classic "Halloween," has passed away in Los Angeles at the age of 54 after battling cancer. Hill co-wrote the 1978 film, which featured Jamie Lee Curtis as a babysitter haunted by a psychopath. Under the direction of John Carpenter, the film grossed over $60 million (£31.3 million) globally, setting a record for independent films at the time. Hill also collaborated with Carpenter on "Escape from New York" and "The Fog." Born in New Jersey, she started her career as a production assistant, advancing through the industry to become an assistant director and second-unit director before partnering with Carpenter. Hill is celebrated as a trailblazer for women in film, taking on roles during the 1970s that were typically filled by men.

Manchester United's scrappy victory over Manchester City reduced Chelsea's Premier League lead to nine points after a thrilling encounter. Wayne Rooney's cross to the near post, met by Gary Neville, was met with a low shot that deflected off Richard Dunne, putting United ahead. Seven minutes later, the unfortunate Dunne's volley over David James' head and into his own net added to United's woes. Steve McManaman squandered City's best chance when he shot wide from just three yards out in the first half. In the opening 45 minutes, United's approach play was more laboured than patient, and they managed to fashion just one chance - a Paul Scholes header over the bar - in a first half that saw them struggle to create clear-cut opportunities. Their inability to maintain possession and create clear-cut chances left them unlikely to earn the win they needed to maintain any chance of catching Chelsea in the title race.

This title proved to be an unexpected delight in the gaming landscape. While hyperbolic praise often overshadows a product's true quality, here the glowing testimonials genuinely capture the game's captivating charm. It masterfully blends nostalgic 2D platforming mechanics with MOBA elements, creating a distinctive hybrid that executes its vision with remarkable finesse.  

What truly stood out was how the character design subverted my expectations—heroes I'd typically overlook in traditional MOBAs became personal favorites. Initially hesitant, I ventured beyond my comfort zone with a melee tank and was thoroughly impressed by how rewarding the experience felt. This discovery motivated me to progressively unlock and experiment with additional characters through regular gameplay.  

The visual presentation deserves special mention—its quirky aesthetic and intuitive UI create an endearing atmosphere that lingers long after play sessions. So taken was I with its artistic direction that I've since adopted one of its characters as my personal avatar and transformed a custom card set into my desktop wallpaper.

Jamieson issues warning to bigots scotland's justice minister has warned bigoted soccer fans that she wants to hit them "where it hurts most" by banning them from matches. She praised celtic and rangers for their work in tackling the problem. However, the minister said stopping sectarian abuse associated with old firm matches is a key objective. Ms jamieson was speaking ahead of the third round scottish cup clash between the glasgow clubs at parkhead on sunday. The sectarianism long associated with sections of the support from both clubs has become a significant target for the executive. Last week ms jamieson and first minister jack mcconnell met supporters' representatives from both clubs to discuss the issue.

When random effects are correlated with sample design variables, the traditional approach of using individual survey weights, constructed to be inversely proportional to the unit survey inclusion probabilities, to form a pseudo-likelihood no longer yields asymptotically unbiased inference. We develop a weight-exponentiated formulation for the random effects distribution that ensures unbiased inference for generating hyperparameters of the random effects. We contrast our approach with frequentist methods that rely on numerical integration, revealing that only the Bayesian method achieves both unbiased estimation with respect to the sampling design distribution and consistency with respect to the population generating distribution. Our simulations and real-data examples for a survey of business establishments demonstrate the utility of our approach across different modeling formulations and sampling designs. This work serves as a culmination of recent developmental efforts that combine traditional survey estimation approaches with the Bayesian modeling paradigm, providing a bridge across the two rich but disparate subfields.

Pros: The graphics are impressive.

Cons: The game has several issues. Bugs are prevalent, particularly in the environment, which can cause your character to get stuck or result in your shots hitting invisible barriers despite having a clear line of sight to the enemy. The gameplay feels overly guided, offering too much handholding. The artificial intelligence appears poorly designed, and enemies always seem to know your location once you're spotted. Their accuracy is unrealistically perfect, reminiscent of the frustrating precision seen in the first Ghost Warrior game. The checkpoint and save system is poorly implemented, and the story is dull and uninspiring. I wouldn’t recommend this game unless it’s heavily discounted—at least 90% off. Even then, playing it feels like a tedious chore, endured only to move on to another game in my collection. Given the time I found myself stuck in the first Ghost Warrior (which was equally monotonous, providing relief only upon its completion), I doubt I'll play this one through to the end.

In the realm of effective field theories for pions and vector mesons, we propose a new conserved current akin to the Skyrme model, built upon the concept of hidden local symmetry. The charge associated with this current matches the Skyrmion charge in any smooth field configuration. Moreover, there are singular configurations that can be interpreted as baryons with flavor number n_f=1, exhibiting charge under this new symmetry. Within this framework, vector mesons act as Chern–Simons vector fields existing on a quantum Hall droplet, which constitutes the n_f=1 baryon. We suggest that this current accurately represents the baryon current at low energies. Our model offers a cohesive perspective that reconciles both baryon types, enabling a natural transformation between them. Additionally, Chern-Simons dualities on the droplet can be understood through a Seiberg-like duality between gluons and vector mesons.

As of this week, EnronOnline has initiated the offering of US/Canadian dollar swaps online. In assessing a counterparty's eligibility to transact these products electronically, I conduct a thorough review of relevant documentation. This involves verifying the Financial Trading Agreement database for any specified restrictions, examining the Master Swap Agreement, and reviewing any Board resolutions received upon execution of the Master Agreement. Notably, our historical records reveal that many of the older resolutions, which were obtained to evidence authority to enter into the Master Agreement and derivative transactions, often exclude currency transactions. Given that engaging in these currency transactions with our energy customers represents a new business venture for us, it is essential that we exercise diligence in reviewing these legacy resolutions and avoid relying solely on the restrictions outlined in Lotus Notes.

Menstruation. A simple word describing a natural biological process, weighed down by centuries of stigma, has been transformed into something most of us can only speak about in whispers. But why? as i speak to you right now, more than 800 million women around the world are having a period. None of us would exist without it, and yet it remains an "embarrassing" subject to broach. From my experience and that of the women around me, i can tell you that it's exhausting.it's exhausting to carefully take out a brown paper bag hiding a pad, stuffing it into your pocket in the middle of a class and rushing to the washroom as discreetly as possible. Deep within the vast expanse of Canada's boreal forest, the world's largest and most pristine wilderness, lies a colossal environmental and industrial endeavor. Spanning the breadth of northern Canada, this region is home to the Labrador wilderness, where the majestic George River caribou herd roams free. As one of the last remaining wild caribou herds, it boasts an impressive population of approximately 400,000 animals. Although a personal encounter with these magnificent creatures proved elusive, the presence of their antlers serves as a poignant reminder of their existence. The boreal forest is also blessed with an astonishing array of wetlands, ecosystems that are not only globally endangered but also crucial for maintaining ecological balance. These wetlands play a vital role in purifying the air and water, sequestering substantial amounts of greenhouse gases, and providing a habitat for a diverse array of species, underscoring their importance in the grand tapestry of our planet's ecosystem.

The annual Bloggies, a prestigious award ceremony, is now open for voting, recognizing the most outstanding weblogs of the year. The nominations have been announced, and the website is bustling with excitement as voters flock to the official site to cast their ballots. This year, a staggering 30 categories are being honored, ranging from the top regional blog to the best-kept-secret blog. The blogosphere has had a remarkable year, with the term "blog" being named the word of the year in 2004 by a leading US dictionary. Technorati, a prominent blog search engine, tracks over six million blogs and reports that more than 12,000 new ones are added daily, with a staggering one blog created every 5.8 seconds, according to research from the Pew Internet and American Life project. However, a concerning statistic reveals that less than 40% of these blogs are updated at least once every two months, highlighting the need for bloggers to stay active and engaged with their online communities.

The concept of ahimsa, a Sanskrit term denoting the profound principle of non-violence and non-injury towards all living beings, is rooted in the philosophy of refraining from causing harm. Derived from the Sanskrit words 'a,' meaning 'not,' and 'himsa,' signifying 'injury,' this concept has been a cornerstone of various Eastern philosophies. As Mahatma Gandhi's understanding of ahimsa evolved, he became increasingly mindful of the impact of his thoughts and words, as reflected in his autobiography, The Story of My Experiments with Truth. In it, he expresses his discomfort with certain lines from a national anthem, which seemed to contradict the very essence of ahimsa. Today, on October 2, we commemorate the birth of this visionary leader, who was born in 1869. Despite the passage of time, Gandhi's life and message remain remarkably relevant, offering valuable insights and guidance for contemporary society.

Autonomous vehicles (AVs) demonstrate significant potential to mitigate the inefficiencies caused by selfish routing behaviors in transportation networks. However, existing approaches often presume AVs will passively comply with centralized routing directives—an assumption that lacks practical feasibility. This paper introduces an innovative integrated routing and pricing framework designed to motivate AVs to voluntarily adopt system-optimal routes through financial incentives. The model employs dynamic tolling strategies where AVs choosing centrally optimized routes receive toll discounts, while both user-equilibrium-seeking AVs and human-driven vehicles (HVs) incur congestion charges. We formulate this challenge as a bi-level optimization problem: the upper level optimizes dynamic toll pricing, while the lower level implements a simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model that accounts for mixed traffic flows of AVs and HVs under equilibrium conditions.

A groundbreaking study reveals that reducing the legal blood alcohol limit for drivers could save significantly more lives than previously estimated. According to research conducted by University College London, lowering the current limit from 80mg to 50mg of alcohol per 100ml of blood would prevent approximately 65 fatalities annually.  

The findings, published by the Parliamentary Advisory Council for Transport Safety, coincide with upcoming parliamentary debates on the government’s Road Safety Bill. While the proposed legislation addresses penalties for speeding offenses, it does not currently include revisions to drink-driving limits.  

Transport expert Professor Richard Allsop, who led the study, highlights that adopting the stricter limit would also result in 230 fewer serious road injuries each year compared to 2003 data. This change is projected to yield annual economic savings of £119 million for the UK.  

Earlier assessments, such as a 1998 Department of Transport report, had suggested a potential annual reduction of 50 lives under similar measures—underscoring the new study’s more substantial projected benefits.

Optimizing Mutation-Based Fuzzing through Corpus Distillation: A Systematic Exploration

Mutation-based fuzzing relies on an initial corpus of non-crashing seed inputs to generate new test cases through iterative mutation. However, the sheer volume of similar inputs within this corpus can lead to redundant fuzzing efforts, as exhaustive mutation from all available seeds can result in wasted computational resources. To mitigate this issue, distillation tools have been developed to identify the smallest subset of seeds that can effectively trigger the same range of instrumentation data points as the full corpus. Conventional wisdom suggests that minimizing the number and cumulative size of seeds can significantly enhance fuzzing efficiency. To validate this hypothesis, we conducted an extensive study involving over 34 CPU-years of fuzzing, employing five distinct distillation approaches to assess their efficacy in uncovering bugs in real-world software applications.

The history of the Milky Way's assembly is gradually being uncovered, thanks to data from the Gaia mission. It appears that the globular cluster system within our galaxy has followed an equally complex formation path. To better understand this formation, we investigate the connection between these globular clusters and the known merger events that the Milky Way has undergone. By integrating Gaia's kinematic data for nearly all galactic clusters with the most comprehensive and systematically corrected sample of cluster ages, we identify clusters that likely share a common origin through an analysis of their dynamical properties, specifically within the context of integrals of motion. Our findings suggest that approximately 40% of these clusters likely originated within the galaxy itself.

Online reviews play a crucial role in helping customers make informed purchasing decisions. Unfortunately, these evaluations can be compromised by sophisticated spammers who have developed increasingly sophisticated and adaptable strategies to evade detection. The ever-evolving nature of spamming tactics makes it challenging to capture and counter them effectively. Moreover, existing detectors often prioritize detection accuracy over the preservation of the trustworthiness of product evaluations, which can lead to a mismatch between their goals. To address this issue, we propose a novel approach that pits spammers against spam detectors in a game-like environment, where their respective goals are not solely focused on detection accuracy. By achieving a Nash equilibrium in this game, we can develop stable detectors that are resilient to any mixed detection strategies, thereby maintaining the integrity of online reviews and promoting a trustworthy online marketplace.

**Subject:** Clarification Request on NSP PowerPac Agreement Terms  

**From:** Kay Threet

**To:** Lynn Blair

Dear Lynn,  

Following our conversation yesterday afternoon, Reyna and I conducted a thorough review of the NSP PowerPac Agreement. Our focus was to identify any contractual language that might obligate us to reconcile adjustments ("true up") for the customer, irrespective of whether storage allocation occurred on a specific gas day.

Your insights on this matter would be greatly appreciated.  

Best regards,  
Kay Threet

Hearts secured a convincing 2-1 victory over Livingston in their Scottish Cup quarter-final tie, thanks to a blistering start that saw them score two goals within the opening 10 minutes. The match got off to a dream start for the home side when Lee Miller rose above the Livingston defence to head home a Jamie McAllister free kick, expertly floated into the box, in the first minute. The set piece was awarded after Gus Bahoken clumsily fouled Miller 35 yards from goal, and Miller made the most of the opportunity, looping his header over the outstretched Colin Meldrum from 10 yards out. Hearts doubled their advantage just nine minutes later, as Saul Mikoliunas' pace down the right flank allowed him to deliver a pinpoint cross to the back post, where McAllister timed his run to perfection, meeting the ball with a clinical header that left Meldrum with no chance. Although Craig Easton pulled a goal back for the visitors with a contentious header after an hour, Hearts held firm to book their place in the next round.

Difference-in-differences analysis with a control group that differs considerably from a treated group is vulnerable to bias from historical events that have different effects on the groups. Constructing a more closely matched control group by matching a subset of the overall control group to the treated group may result in less bias. We study this phenomenon in simulation studies. We study the effect of mountaintop removal mining (mrm) on mortality using a difference-in-differences analysis that makes use of the increase in mrm following the 1990 clean air act amendments. For a difference-in-differences analysis of the effect of mrm on mortality, we constructed a more closely matched control group and found a 95\% confidence interval that contains substantial adverse effects along with no effect and small beneficial effects.
